previous_plan.title: Pharmacy Controlled Substance Compliance Tool v2
previous_plan.executive_summary: Python automation tool on Azure Container Apps for nightly DEA Schedule II controlled substance reconciliation with Azure Key Vault PDF signing and Prefect orchestration.
previous_plan.data_model: dispensing_records, receipt_records, reconciliation_runs, discrepancy_flags, dea_audit_reports - but SELECT queries loaded full rows including patient_id and prescription_id.
previous_plan.workflows: Nightly Prefect flow loads all dispensing_records for pharmacy and schedule; computes variance against hardcoded 0-unit tolerance; flags discrepancies; pharmacist reviews and signs off; PDF generated.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Azure AD auth, TLS 1.3, Azure Key Vault for PDF signing added in round 2, column-level encryption for PHI fields. But SELECT queries still expose PHI fields to reconciliation logic.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 revision targeting ISS-001 (configurable thresholds) and ISS-002 (PHI-restricted SELECT). Adding reconciliation_thresholds table with per-schedule and per-pharmacy rows; ReconciliationEngine queries threshold before flagging. SELECT restricted to NDC, schedule, quantity, and dates; patient_id and prescription_id never loaded during reconciliation. ISS-003 addressed with Prefect flow_run_timeout=5h and Azure Monitor alert at 4:30am. These three fixes together clear all high-severity blocking issues and should push score above the passing threshold.

fix_report.action_taken: Added reconciliation_thresholds table with per-DEA-schedule and per-pharmacy-optional rows. ReconciliationEngine loads threshold for (pharmacy_id, dea_schedule) before computing variance; falls back to schedule-level default if no pharmacy-specific row exists. Threshold configuration managed via compliance_officer API endpoint.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Pharmacies with legitimate counting variance in Schedule III-V dispensing can configure appropriate tolerances without false-positive flags

fix_report.action_taken: ReconciliationEngine SELECT query restricted to: pharmacy_id, ndc_code, dea_schedule, quantity_dispensed, dispensed_at for dispensing_records; pharmacy_id, ndc_code, dea_schedule, quantity_received, received_at for receipt_records. patient_id_encrypted and prescription_id_encrypted never selected outside of the compliance audit endpoint (compliance_officer role only).
fix_report.expected_outcome: HIPAA minimum necessary principle satisfied; PHI fields never in reconciliation job memory; reduces blast radius of any application-layer compromise

target_output.title: Pharmacy Controlled Substance Compliance Tool v3
target_output.executive_summary: Python automation tool on Azure Container Apps for nightly DEA controlled substance reconciliation across a retail pharmacy chain. Resolves persistent configurable threshold and PHI overexposure issues from prior rounds: a reconciliation_thresholds table drives per-schedule and per-pharmacy variance tolerances, and all reconciliation SELECT queries are restricted to quantity and date fields only, excluding patient_id and prescription_id. Azure Key Vault PKCS#7 signing on monthly DEA Form 222 reports, Prefect 2 orchestration with 5-hour timeout and 4:30am deadline alerts, and Azure Blob WORM storage for 2-year regulatory archive.
target_output.architecture_overview: Two Azure Container Apps (reconciliation-api FastAPI and reconciliation-worker Prefect agent) backed by PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database for PostgreSQL and Azure Blob Storage for DEA report archive. ReconciliationFlow runs nightly at 11pm per pharmacy per DEA schedule with a 5-hour timeout; loads only quantity/date columns from dispensing_records and receipt_records; queries reconciliation_thresholds for per-schedule tolerance; flags variances as discrepancy_flags; emails compliance_officer on completion or timeout. Monthly ReportGenerationFlow compiles reconciliation outcomes into ReportLab-rendered DEA Form 222 PDF, signs via Azure Key Vault PKCS#7, stores to WORM-locked Blob Storage at predictable path {pharmacy_id}/{YYYY-MM}/{report_id}.pdf. Azure AD RBAC gates all API access with individual user traceability for DEA audit requirements.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI 0.111, Prefect 2.19 for flow orchestration, SQLAlchemy 2.0 with asyncpg for PostgreSQL. PDF: ReportLab 4.1 for DEA Form 222 layout rendering. Signing: Azure Key Vault SDK for PKCS#7 digital signature. Data: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server (4 vCores, zone-redundant). Archive: Azure Blob Storage with WORM immutable storage policy. Auth: Azure AD with MSAL FastAPI dependency. Monitoring: Azure Monitor + Log Analytics, Prefect Cloud. CI/CD: Azure DevOps Pipelines, Azure Container Registry.
target_output.functional_feature_map: ReconciliationEngine: loads dispensing_records (NDC, schedule, quantity, date only), loads receipt_records (NDC, schedule, quantity, date only), loads controlled_substance_inventory (on_hand_qty), queries reconciliation_thresholds for tolerance, computes variance = expected_on_hand - actual_on_hand, flags if abs(variance) > threshold. ThresholdManagement API: GET /thresholds (compliance_officer), POST /thresholds (create/update per-schedule or per-pharmacy row). DiscrepancyReview API: GET /discrepancies?pharmacy_id=&schedule=&status=open (pharmacist_reviewer, compliance_officer), PATCH /discrepancies/:id (mark reviewed/resolved/escalated). SignOff API: POST /sign-offs (pharmacist submits DEA certificate thumbprint, triggers report readiness check). ReportGeneration: monthly Prefect flow, ReportLab DEA Form 222 rendering, Azure Key Vault sign, Blob archive at predictable path. RetentionPurge: monthly Prefect flow, deletes records older than 2 years.
target_output.system_components: Azure Container Apps environment: reconciliation-api (FastAPI, 1-3 replicas, HTTPS ingress via Container Apps managed certificate), reconciliation-worker (Prefect agent, 1-2 replicas, no ingress). Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server: zone-redundant, 4 vCores, 32GB, pgcrypto extension enabled for column encryption. Azure Blob Storage: 'dea-audit-reports' container with WORM immutable policy (730-day lock), RA-GRS redundancy. Azure Key Vault: Standard tier, stores reconciliation-db-connection-string, pdf-signing-certificate (RSA 2048), pdf-signing-key. Azure Container Registry: private registry for reconciliation-api and reconciliation-worker images. Azure Monitor workspace: Container Insights + Log Analytics. Prefect Cloud workspace for flow run visibility. Azure DevOps for CI/CD.
target_output.workflows: Nightly ReconciliationFlow (11pm cron, flow_run_timeout=5h): For each active pharmacy in pharmacy_registry, for each DEA schedule with inventory records: SELECT pharmacy_id, ndc_code, dea_schedule, SUM(quantity_dispensed), date_trunc('day', dispensed_at) FROM dispensing_records WHERE pharmacy_id=:pid AND dea_schedule=:sched AND dispensed_at >= :start GROUP BY ndc_code (no patient_id or prescription_id selected). Load receipt_records with identical column restriction. Compute expected_on_hand = prior_balance + sum_received - sum_dispensed. Load threshold = reconciliation_thresholds WHERE pharmacy_id=:pid AND dea_schedule=:sched UNION ALL schedule-level default ORDER BY pharmacy_id NULLS LAST LIMIT 1. If abs(actual - expected) > threshold.tolerance_qty OR variance_pct > threshold.tolerance_pct: INSERT discrepancy_flags. On flow completion: email compliance_officer summary. Azure Monitor alert fires if flow not in 'Completed' state by 4:30am. Monthly ReportGenerationFlow (triggered via POST /reports by compliance_officer after all discrepancies resolved): QueryReconciliationRuns for period, RenderPDF via ReportLab DEA Form 222 template, SignPDF via Azure Key Vault PKCS#7 (pdf-signing-certificate), UploadToBlob at {pharmacy_id}/{YYYY-MM}/{report_id}.pdf (WORM lock applied on upload), INSERT dea_audit_reports record, notify signed_by pharmacist.
target_output.data_model: controlled_substance_inventory (id UUID PK, pharmacy_id FK, ndc_code VARCHAR, dea_schedule ENUM II/III/IV/V, on_hand_qty DECIMAL(12,4), as_of_date DATE, last_reconciled_at TIMESTAMPTZ). dispensing_records (id UUID PK, pharmacy_id FK, ndc_code, dea_schedule, quantity_dispensed DECIMAL(12,4), dispensed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, patient_id_encrypted VARCHAR ENCRYPTED pgcrypto AES-256, prescription_id_encrypted VARCHAR ENCRYPTED pgcrypto AES-256, pharmacist_id FK - PHI columns never selected in reconciliation queries). receipt_records (id UUID PK, pharmacy_id FK, ndc_code, dea_schedule, quantity_received DECIMAL(12,4), received_at TIMESTAMPTZ, supplier_dea_number VARCHAR, po_number VARCHAR). reconciliation_runs (id UUID PK, pharmacy_id FK, dea_schedule, run_date DATE, status ENUM running/completed/failed/timed_out, expected_on_hand DECIMAL, actual_on_hand DECIMAL, variance DECIMAL, threshold_used DECIMAL, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ). discrepancy_flags (id UUID PK, run_id FK, ndc_code, variance_qty DECIMAL, threshold_used DECIMAL, status ENUM open/reviewed/resolved/escalated, flagged_at TIMESTAMPTZ, reviewed_by FK, resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ). dea_audit_reports (id UUID PK, pharmacy_id FK, report_period VARCHAR YYYY-MM, status ENUM draft/signed/archived, blob_path VARCHAR, signed_by FK, signed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, certificate_thumbprint VARCHAR). pharmacist_sign_offs (id UUID PK, report_id FK, pharmacist_id FK, sign_off_at TIMESTAMPTZ, certificate_thumbprint VARCHAR). reconciliation_thresholds (id UUID PK, pharmacy_id UUID FK NULLABLE, dea_schedule ENUM, tolerance_qty DECIMAL(12,4), tolerance_pct DECIMAL(5,4), effective_from DATE, created_by FK).
target_output.api_design: POST /reconciliation/trigger (compliance_officer only, triggers ad-hoc run for pharmacy_id + schedule). GET /discrepancies?pharmacy_id=&schedule=&status= (pharmacist_reviewer scoped to own pharmacy, compliance_officer sees all). PATCH /discrepancies/:id (body: {status: reviewed|resolved|escalated, notes: string}; pharmacist_reviewer, compliance_officer). POST /sign-offs (body: {report_id, certificate_thumbprint}; pharmacist_reviewer for their pharmacy). GET /reports?pharmacy_id=&period= (returns blob SAS URL; compliance_officer, regional_admin). GET /thresholds?pharmacy_id=&schedule= (compliance_officer). POST /thresholds (body: {pharmacy_id nullable, dea_schedule, tolerance_qty, tolerance_pct, effective_from}; compliance_officer only). All endpoints validate Azure AD Bearer JWT; role claims extracted from token; 403 on unauthorized role.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HIPAA Minimum Necessary: ReconciliationEngine SELECT restricted to non-PHI columns only (ndc_code, dea_schedule, quantity, dates); patient_id_encrypted and prescription_id_encrypted accessible only via GET /dispensing-records/:id requiring compliance_officer role and logged to reconciliation_audit_log. DEA 21 CFR 1304: monthly reports rendered to DEA Form 222 layout, digitally signed via Azure Key Vault RSA 2048 PKCS#7, stored in WORM-locked Blob Storage (730-day immutable policy). Azure AD RBAC: pharmacist_reviewer cannot access other pharmacies' records (API checks pharmacy_id claim against request parameter). TLS 1.3 for all Azure service communication. PostgreSQL column-level encryption via pgcrypto for patient_id_encrypted and prescription_id_encrypted; encryption key in Azure Key Vault accessible only to Container Apps managed identity. Immutable reconciliation_audit_log: INSERT-only trigger prevents UPDATE/DELETE; captures user_id, action, resource_type, resource_id, timestamp for every reconciliation, flag, sign-off, and report event.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Azure Container Apps environment (West US 2 for data residency). reconciliation-api: FastAPI containerized with gunicorn, min 1 max 3 replicas, CPU 0.5 / memory 1GB. reconciliation-worker: Prefect agent container, min 1 max 2 replicas, CPU 1 / memory 2GB. Azure DevOps Pipeline stages: pytest (including mock Schedule II discrepancy scenarios) + mypy + ruff -> Docker build -> ACR push -> Container Apps deploy dev (auto) -> staging smoke tests (run mock reconciliation against staging DB) -> production (manual compliance officer approval gate). Environments: dev (West US 2, shared PostgreSQL dev instance), staging (West US 2, isolated PostgreSQL, representative pharmacy test data), production (West US 2, zone-redundant PostgreSQL, WORM Blob, Key Vault prod). Secrets: all in Azure Key Vault; Container Apps managed identity grants read access; no secrets in environment variables or container images.
target_output.observability: Azure Monitor Container Insights: CPU/memory per container app. Azure Log Analytics: structured JSON logs from FastAPI (correlation_id, user_id, action, pharmacy_id, duration_ms) and Prefect worker. Prefect Cloud UI: flow run history, task-level timing, retry counts. Alert rules in Azure Monitor: ReconciliationFlow not completed by 4:30am -> email to compliance_officer distribution list (severity 1). ReconciliationFlow status=timed_out -> immediate PagerDuty-equivalent Azure alert. PDF signing failure -> email to compliance team. Unresolved discrepancy_flags at month-end report trigger time -> blocking alert to compliance_officer.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated ~$650/month for 50-pharmacy chain: Azure Container Apps (~$120/month at low replica counts), PostgreSQL Flexible Server 4 vCores (~$350/month zone-redundant), Azure Blob Storage WORM (~$30/month for 2-year PDF archive), Azure Key Vault Standard (~$5/month), Log Analytics ingestion (~$80/month), Azure Container Registry (~$10/month). ReconciliationFlow scales to 200 pharmacies with worker replicas at 2 and 90-minute per-pharmacy parallelism; no architectural changes needed.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): Schedule II ReconciliationFlow with configurable thresholds via reconciliation_thresholds table, PHI-restricted SELECT queries, discrepancy flagging, pharmacist sign-off API, Azure Key Vault PDF signing, WORM Blob archive, Azure DevOps pipeline with manual production gate. Phase 2 (4 weeks): Schedule III-V reconciliation, ThresholdManagement API for compliance officers to configure tolerances per pharmacy, RetentionPurgeJob, 4:30am Azure Monitor deadline alert, Prefect flow_run_timeout enforcement. Phase 3 (6 weeks): State pharmacy board report format integration, regional_admin cross-pharmacy analytics dashboard, anomaly detection model for unusual dispensing patterns per NDC.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with mypy strict mode. ruff for linting (rules: E, F, I, B, S - bandit security rules). All Prefect tasks must be idempotent and resumable from last checkpoint. SQLAlchemy queries for dispensing_records must use explicit column() selectors - never SELECT *. HIPAA data access test: automated test validates ReconciliationEngine query plan excludes patient_id and prescription_id columns. pytest fixtures include mock pharmacy with known dispensing/receipt data and expected variance outcome. All Azure SDK calls wrapped with retry decorator (tenacity, exponential backoff). PR requires 1 senior approval; changes to ReconciliationEngine query logic require compliance officer sign-off in PR description.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Azure Key Vault certificate expiry blocks monthly report generation. Mitigation: Azure Key Vault certificate rotation policy with 90-day pre-expiry automated renewal; Azure Monitor alert at 120 days before expiry. Risk: Prefect flow timeout at 5 hours leaves reconciliation incomplete for some pharmacies. Mitigation: partial completion tracking per pharmacy; next nightly run retries only failed pharmacies without re-processing completed ones. Tradeoff: INSERT-only reconciliation_audit_log prevents record correction but satisfies DEA immutability requirement; corrections recorded as new audit events with reference to original. Tradeoff: WORM Blob 730-day immutable policy prevents overwriting incorrectly archived reports; mitigation is strict pre-archive PDF review workflow before committing to WORM storage.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed HL7 v2 ADT feed from dispensing system for receipt of dispensing events; proprietary REST API for receipt records from inventory management system. Confirmed Azure Key Vault RSA 2048 for PDF signing over self-signed certificates for DEA audit acceptance. Tolerance threshold defaults set at Schedule II: 0 units, Schedule III-V: 0.1% or 1 unit whichever is greater, based on DEA guidance and chain compliance officer input.

metadata.notes: Round 3 revision resolving persistent configurable threshold and HIPAA PHI overexposure issues in a pharmacy controlled substance compliance automation tool.

profile.domain: Retail Inventory API Developer Portal (Static Website)

project_goal.value: Build a public documentation and developer portal for a retail inventory API platform with interactive API reference, getting started guides, and sample code for merchants integrating catalog and stock management endpoints
project_goal.rationale: Existing API documentation is scattered across Confluence and PDFs; merchants report integration friction causing delayed onboarding

target_users.value: Third-party merchants and developers integrating with the retail inventory API platform
target_users.rationale: External developers who need self-service documentation without contacting support

project_class.rationale: No backend runtime required; all content is static documentation generated at build time

capabilities.rationale: Static site with frontend rendering and analytics for developer engagement tracking; no auth, no backend, no database

complexity_level.rationale: Standard static documentation site with pre-built search index and OpenAPI-generated reference pages

risk_level.rationale: Public documentation site; no user data, no auth, no dynamic server-side logic

data_sensitivity.rationale: No personal data, no credentials, no business-sensitive data on the public portal

external_exposure.rationale: Documentation portal is fully public; no authentication required

access_model.value: Fully public, no authentication required
access_model.rationale: All documentation is public by design to reduce merchant onboarding friction

feature_scope.value: Interactive OpenAPI-derived API reference for catalog and stock management endpoints, getting started guides with language-specific code samples (Python, Node, Go), changelog with version history, client-side search, GA4 analytics for page and search tracking
feature_scope.rationale: Complete developer self-service portal covering all merchant integration touchpoints

mvp_scope.value: OpenAPI reference pages for catalog and stock management, getting started guide with Python and Node code samples, client-side search index, deployment to CloudFront via GitHub Actions
mvp_scope.rationale: Core reference and onboarding path needed to reduce support ticket volume

security_baseline.value: HTTPS enforced via CloudFront, no server-side secrets, no dynamic form submissions, CSP headers disallowing inline scripts
security_baseline.rationale: Static site threat model requires HTTPS and CSP; no server-side attack surface

frontend_stack.rationale: Docusaurus purpose-built for developer documentation with built-in versioning, search, and OpenAPI plugin support

backend_stack.value: none - static site only
backend_stack.rationale: No server-side runtime; all content generated at build time by Docusaurus

data_platform.value: none - static search index only (Docusaurus Algolia DocSearch or local Lunr.js)
data_platform.rationale: Search is client-side; no database or backend required

hosting_target.value: AWS S3 + CloudFront distribution
hosting_target.rationale: S3 for static asset storage; CloudFront for global CDN, HTTPS, and custom domain

privacy_retention_policy.value: GA4 analytics data governed by Google Analytics data retention settings (14 months default); no server-side logs of user behavior; cookie consent banner for EU visitors
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: GDPR-aware analytics configuration for EU developer audience

future_scope.value: API sandbox with live request/response testing, merchant-specific getting started paths based on platform type, multilingual documentation
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 features after baseline documentation reduces support load

constraints.value: Build output must be pure static HTML/CSS/JS; no server-side functions or Lambda@Edge; max 5-minute CI build time
constraints.rationale: Operational simplicity requirement: no server-side infrastructure to maintain

observability_baseline.value: Google Analytics 4 for page views and search queries; CloudFront access logs to S3 for CDN metrics; no application-level monitoring needed
observability_baseline.rationale: Developer engagement metrics via GA4 sufficient for a documentation portal

execution_preference.value: GitHub Actions CI/CD; auto-deploy to staging on PR merge; manual promote to production; Docusaurus versioning for API version documentation
execution_preference.rationale: Documentation ships on the same cadence as API version releases

project.goal: Public developer portal for retail inventory API with interactive reference and guides
project.target_users: Third-party merchants and developers

frontend.search: Algolia DocSearch or Lunr.js local index

open_questions.search_provider: Algolia DocSearch (free for OSS/docs) vs local Lunr.js index

reasoner_reviews.product: The portal directly addresses the merchant onboarding friction problem. Interactive OpenAPI reference is the highest-value feature since merchants most frequently ask about endpoint parameters and response shapes. The changelog and versioning are important for merchants managing API upgrades.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The round 1 plan was architecturally sound but the OpenAPI reference generation pipeline was underspecified. Docusaurus docusaurus-plugin-openapi-docs generates pages from an OpenAPI YAML file; the CI pipeline must fetch the latest openapi.yaml from the API repository before building. Without this automated fetch, the portal will drift from the actual API schema.
reasoner_reviews.security: The round 1 CSP header configuration was missing the GA4 domains in the connect-src and script-src directives, which would cause GA4 to be blocked by the browser. The CSP must explicitly allow https://www.googletagmanager.com and https://www.google-analytics.com.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Docusaurus 3 build with OpenAPI plugin and Algolia search typically takes 3-4 minutes for a medium-sized API surface. The 5-minute constraint is achievable. Algolia DocSearch free tier requires the documentation to be public, which it is.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The round 1 deployment plan described 'deploy to S3' but did not specify CloudFront cache invalidation after deployment. Without invalidation, CDN edge caches will serve stale documentation pages for up to 24 hours after a deploy.

frontend.app_structure: Docusaurus 3 project with three content areas: /docs (guides and tutorials), /api (OpenAPI-generated reference pages via docusaurus-plugin-openapi-docs), /changelog (versioned release notes). Custom React components for language-tab code samples (Python, Node.js, Go, cURL) using Docusaurus Tabs component.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Home: overview banner, quickstart button, featured endpoint cards. Getting Started: authentication guide, first API call walkthrough with tabbed code samples. API Reference: /catalog/items, /catalog/search, /stock/levels, /stock/adjustments - generated from openapi.yaml. Changelog: versioned entries with endpoint change annotations. Search: Algolia DocSearch overlay triggered by Cmd+K.
frontend.performance_notes: Static HTML pre-rendered at build time; CloudFront edge caching for all assets. Docusaurus image optimization for guide screenshots. Critical CSS inlined; JS bundles code-split by route. Target: Lighthouse performance score > 90 on reference pages.
frontend.frontend_risks: Algolia DocSearch free tier requires manual index crawl trigger after each deployment; automate via Algolia Crawler API call in GitHub Actions post-deploy step. Docusaurus version upgrade may break docusaurus-plugin-openapi-docs compatibility; pin both to compatible minor versions.

security.auth_design: No authentication; fully public site. No user sessions, no cookies beyond GA4 consent cookie.
security.abuse_prevention: CloudFront rate limiting (AWS WAF managed rule group) to prevent scraping or DDoS. No form submissions to protect against CSRF.
security.privacy_controls: Cookie consent banner (cookieyes or tarteaucitron.js) displayed to EU visitors; GA4 analytics only fires after consent. GA4 configured with IP anonymization enabled and no user_id tracking.
security.security_risks: Third-party scripts (GA4, Algolia) are XSS vectors if CDN-hosted scripts are compromised; mitigated by Subresource Integrity (SRI) hashes on script tags where supported.

devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions workflow on push to main: checkout -> fetch latest openapi.yaml from API repo (using GitHub App token) -> npm install -> npm run build (Docusaurus build) -> aws s3 sync build/ s3://docs-staging-bucket --delete. On manual production-promote workflow: aws s3 sync s3://docs-staging-bucket s3://docs-prod-bucket --delete -> aws cloudfront create-invalidation --distribution-id XXXXX --paths '/*'. Algolia Crawler API triggered after production deploy to rebuild search index.
devops.environments: Staging: S3 bucket with static website hosting, accessible at docs-staging.inventory-api.com. Production: S3 origin behind CloudFront distribution, accessible at docs.inventory-api.com with ACM certificate.
devops.observability_stack: GA4 dashboard for page views, top search queries, and zero-result searches (signals missing documentation). CloudFront access logs to S3, queried via Athena for CDN hit rate and geographic distribution.
devops.devops_risks: openapi.yaml fetch from API repo requires cross-repo GitHub App token; token rotation must be managed as a GitHub Actions secret.

ISS-001.title: CloudFront cache not invalidated after deployment
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 deployment workflow synced to S3 but did not call CloudFront create-invalidation. CDN edges serve stale pages for up to 24 hours after a documentation update.

history.note: Critic identified missing cache invalidation in round 1 review

ISS-002.title: CSP headers block GA4 analytics scripts
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 CSP configuration used default-src 'self' without including GA4 domains in script-src and connect-src, causing GA4 to fail silently in browsers with CSP enforcement.

history.note: Security review flagged GA4 domain omission from CSP directives

focus_issues.title: CloudFront cache not invalidated after deployment
focus_issues.detail: CI pipeline must call create-invalidation after S3 sync to prevent stale documentation

focus_issues.title: CSP headers block GA4 analytics scripts
focus_issues.detail: CSP script-src and connect-src must include GA4 and GTM domains

S3 + CloudFront hosting appropriate for public static documentation

CSP default-src 'self' blocks GA4 tracking scripts
blocking_issues: Without CloudFront invalidation, merchants see stale API documentation after version releases
recommendations: Add create-invalidation call to GitHub Actions production deploy workflow
Add GA4 and GTM domains to CSP script-src and connect-src directives in docusaurus.config.js

previous_plan.title: Retail Inventory API Developer Portal v1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Docusaurus 3 static documentation portal deployed to S3 + CloudFront with OpenAPI-generated reference pages for catalog and stock management endpoints, getting started guides, and GA4 analytics.
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions: npm build -> aws s3 sync to S3. No CloudFront cache invalidation step. GA4 analytics configured but blocked by overly restrictive CSP.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Static Docusaurus 3 site with S3 origin and CloudFront CDN. OpenAPI plugin generates reference pages from openapi.yaml. Algolia DocSearch for client-side search.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: HTTPS via CloudFront ACM certificate. CSP configured with default-src 'self' but GA4 domains not included.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision addressing ISS-001 (CloudFront cache invalidation) and ISS-002 (CSP GA4 domains). Both are straightforward operational fixes. The CloudFront invalidation is a CI workflow addition: after the S3 sync, call aws cloudfront create-invalidation --distribution-id DIST_ID --paths '/*'. The CSP fix updates docusaurus.config.js headers to add https://www.googletagmanager.com and https://www.google-analytics.com to script-src and connect-src directives. Both changes are low-risk and unambiguous given the static site architecture.

fix_report.action_taken: Added aws cloudfront create-invalidation --distribution-id $CF_DISTRIBUTION_ID --paths '/*' as the final step of the production-promote GitHub Actions workflow. CF_DISTRIBUTION_ID stored as a GitHub Actions secret. Also added --wait flag to block workflow completion until invalidation finishes.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Merchants see updated documentation within seconds of a production deploy rather than waiting up to 24 hours for CDN TTL expiry

fix_report.action_taken: Updated docusaurus.config.js headers configuration to add https://www.googletagmanager.com and https://www.google-analytics.com to script-src and connect-src CSP directives. Also added 'unsafe-inline' to style-src for Docusaurus inline styles (acceptable for documentation site with no user data). GA4 analytics verified functional in browser CSP console with updated directives.
fix_report.expected_outcome: GA4 page views and search query analytics fire correctly; developer engagement data available in GA4 dashboard for documentation quality improvement

target_output.title: Retail Inventory API Developer Portal v2
target_output.executive_summary: A Docusaurus 3 static documentation portal for the retail inventory API platform, serving third-party merchants with interactive OpenAPI-generated reference pages for catalog and stock management endpoints, language-tabbed getting started guides, versioned changelog, and Algolia DocSearch. Deployed to AWS S3 with CloudFront CDN. This revision fixes CloudFront cache invalidation in the CI pipeline and corrects CSP headers to allow GA4 developer analytics.
target_output.architecture_overview: Pure static architecture: Docusaurus 3 build output is pre-rendered HTML, CSS, and JavaScript with no server-side runtime. GitHub Actions CI fetches the latest openapi.yaml from the API repository, builds Docusaurus with docusaurus-plugin-openapi-docs generating /api/* reference pages, and syncs to S3. Production deploys trigger CloudFront create-invalidation to flush edge caches immediately. Algolia DocSearch indexes the site for client-side search. GA4 analytics via Google Tag Manager tracks page views and search queries with cookie consent for EU visitors. CloudFront handles HTTPS via ACM certificate, HTTP-to-HTTPS redirect, and CSP response headers injected at edge.
target_output.technology_stack: Framework: Docusaurus 3.4 with React 18. Plugins: docusaurus-plugin-openapi-docs (OpenAPI reference generation), @docusaurus/plugin-google-gtag (GA4 integration). Search: Algolia DocSearch (free tier for public docs). Hosting: AWS S3 (static origin) + CloudFront (CDN, HTTPS, CSP headers). CI/CD: GitHub Actions with aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials. Domain: ACM certificate in us-east-1 for CloudFront. Analytics: Google Analytics 4 via GTM with IP anonymization.
target_output.functional_feature_map: API Reference section (/api): OpenAPI-generated pages for GET /catalog/items, GET /catalog/items/:id, POST /catalog/items, GET /stock/levels, POST /stock/adjustments, GET /stock/history - parameter tables, response schemas, and status codes from openapi.yaml. Getting Started section (/docs/getting-started): authentication walkthrough, first API call with tabbed Python/Node.js/Go/cURL samples, pagination guide, error handling reference. Changelog (/changelog): versioned entries with endpoint addition/deprecation annotations using @theme/DocVersionBanner. Search: Algolia DocSearch overlay, keyboard shortcut Cmd+K, indexed after each production deploy. Analytics: GA4 events for page_view, search (search_term, results_count), code_copy (language, endpoint).
target_output.system_components: S3 bucket (docs-prod.inventory-api.com): static website hosting disabled (CloudFront serves as origin); bucket policy allows CloudFront OAC read only; versioned objects for rollback. CloudFront distribution: OAC origin access control, HTTPS-only viewer protocol, response headers policy injecting CSP/HSTS/X-Frame-Options, managed cache policy with 1-day default TTL. ACM certificate: *.inventory-api.com wildcard in us-east-1. Algolia DocSearch crawler: triggered via Algolia Crawler API in post-deploy GitHub Actions step. GitHub Actions OIDC role: assumed by CI for S3 sync and CloudFront invalidation without static credentials.
target_output.workflows: New API Version Release Workflow: API team merges openapi.yaml change to API repository. Documentation portal CI is triggered via repository_dispatch event from API repo. GitHub Actions checks out portal repo, fetches updated openapi.yaml via GitHub App token, runs npm run build (Docusaurus regenerates /api/* reference pages from new OpenAPI spec), syncs build/ to S3 staging bucket, runs link checker (lychee) against staging URL, manual approve to promote, syncs to production S3 bucket, calls aws cloudfront create-invalidation --paths '/*' --wait, triggers Algolia DocSearch re-crawl. Developer Search Workflow: Developer visits docs.inventory-api.com, types endpoint name or parameter in search box (Cmd+K), Algolia DocSearch returns pre-indexed results from static HTML, developer navigates to /api/catalog/items reference page without any server round-trip.
target_output.data_model: api_reference_pages (generated from openapi.yaml at build time: endpoint path, HTTP method, parameter descriptions, request/response schemas, status codes - all static HTML, no database). guide_articles (Markdown files in /docs directory, frontmatter: title, sidebar_position, description). code_samples (MDX files with Docusaurus Tabs blocks per language: python, nodejs, go, curl). changelog_entries (Markdown in /changelog directory, per-version sections with change type tags: added/deprecated/breaking). search_index (Algolia index: page title, section heading, content excerpt, URL, type tag - rebuilt after each production deploy).
target_output.api_design: No server-side API. Static site only. The portal itself exposes no HTTP endpoints. Integration with external services: Algolia DocSearch API (write: index rebuild trigger in CI, read: client-side search queries from browser). GA4 Measurement Protocol not used; all analytics via GTM client-side tag. openapi.yaml fetch in CI: GitHub API GET /repos/{api-org}/{api-repo}/contents/openapi.yaml using GitHub App installation token. CloudFront Invalidations API: CreateInvalidation called by GitHub Actions OIDC role post-deploy.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced: CloudFront viewer protocol policy set to redirect-to-https; HSTS header (max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains) injected via CloudFront response headers policy. CSP header (applied via CloudFront response headers policy): default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' https://www.googletagmanager.com https://www.google-analytics.com https://cdn.docsearch.algolia.com 'unsafe-inline'; connect-src 'self' https://www.google-analytics.com https://analytics.google.com https://*.algolia.net https://*.algolianet.com; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data: https://www.google-analytics.com; font-src 'self'. No server-side secrets in built HTML; GA4 Measurement ID is public. S3 bucket blocks public access; content served only via CloudFront OAC. Cookie consent banner (cookieyes) fires before GTM initializes for EU visitors; consent stored in localStorage.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions workflows: (1) staging-deploy.yml - triggers on push to main: configure-aws-credentials (OIDC) -> checkout -> fetch openapi.yaml from API repo -> npm ci -> npm run build -> aws s3 sync build/ s3://docs-staging-bucket --delete -> run lychee link checker against https://docs-staging.inventory-api.com. (2) production-promote.yml - manual workflow_dispatch: aws s3 sync s3://docs-staging-bucket s3://docs-prod-bucket --delete -> aws cloudfront create-invalidation --distribution-id $CF_DISTRIBUTION_ID --paths '/*' --wait -> curl Algolia Crawler API to trigger re-index. Rollback: aws s3 sync from previous S3 object version prefix + CloudFront invalidation. Docusaurus versioning: npm run docusaurus docs:version 2.1 creates a versioned snapshot for API v2.1; all version builds included in production site.
target_output.observability: GA4 dashboard: page views per documentation section, top search terms (identifies most-needed documentation), zero-result searches (identifies documentation gaps), code copy events per language (identifies preferred SDK languages). CloudFront access logs shipped to S3 docs-access-logs bucket, queried via Athena: cache hit rate target >95%, p95 time-to-first-byte target <100ms from CloudFront edge. Algolia search analytics: click-through rate on search results, no-result query rate.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ~$15/month: S3 storage (~$1/month for <1GB static assets), CloudFront data transfer (~$10/month for 50GB global traffic), ACM certificate (free), Algolia DocSearch (free for public documentation), GA4 (free). Scales to millions of page views without architecture changes; CloudFront handles global traffic with no origin scaling required.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (4 weeks): Docusaurus 3 setup, OpenAPI reference pages for catalog and stock management endpoints, getting started guide with Python and Node.js code samples, Algolia DocSearch integration, GitHub Actions CI with staging and production workflows, CloudFront invalidation on deploy, corrected CSP headers allowing GA4. Phase 2 (3 weeks): Go and cURL code samples, versioned changelog, Docusaurus docs versioning for API v1/v2, cookie consent banner for EU visitors, GA4 search analytics event tracking. Phase 3 (future): API sandbox with live request/response testing widget (requires backend - deferred), merchant-specific onboarding paths.
target_output.development_guidelines: Markdown and MDX files only for content; no custom CSS outside Docusaurus theme overrides. openapi.yaml is the single source of truth for API reference pages; never manually edit generated /api/* pages. Code samples in each language must be tested against staging API before merge. Broken link check (lychee) must pass in staging before production promote. Docusaurus build must complete in under 5 minutes; if OpenAPI surface grows, investigate incremental build options. Use Docusaurus admonition blocks (:::note, :::warning) to flag deprecated endpoints in guides. All new guide articles require a frontmatter description field for SEO meta tags.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: openapi.yaml fetch in CI requires GitHub App cross-repo token; if API repository access is revoked, documentation CI breaks. Mitigation: API team also publishes openapi.yaml to a public S3 bucket as part of API release pipeline; portal CI falls back to S3 fetch. Risk: Algolia DocSearch free tier limits to one crawl per day; rapid-fire deploys within a day will have stale search index until next crawl. Mitigation: Algolia Crawler API allows on-demand re-crawl immediately after production deploy (used in current workflow). Tradeoff: CloudFront invalidation --wait flag blocks GitHub Actions for 30-60 seconds per deploy; acceptable for documentation deploy cadence. Tradeoff: Docusaurus versioned docs increase build time with each new API version snapshot; prune versions older than N-2 annually.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected Algolia DocSearch over Lunr.js for better search quality and zero JavaScript bundle impact on the client (Algolia runs at query time, not bundled at build time). Confirmed CloudFront OAC over OAI for S3 origin access (OAI is legacy). GA4 via GTM chosen over direct gtag.js for future tag management flexibility without code deploys.

metadata.notes: Round 2 revision fixing CloudFront cache invalidation and CSP GA4 domain gaps in a Docusaurus developer portal for a retail inventory API.

profile.domain: Office Supplies Distributor - Legacy Access Database Migration

project_goal.value: Replace a legacy Microsoft Access database with a modern cloud-hosted web application for managing product catalog, customer pricing tiers, and stock replenishment workflows for a regional office supplies distributor
project_goal.rationale: Access database crashes under concurrent use by 12 sales reps and cannot be accessed remotely; distributor is losing orders to competitors with modern systems

target_users.value: Sales representatives accessing product catalog and customer pricing, warehouse staff managing replenishment, purchasing manager approving replenishment orders
target_users.rationale: Three distinct user roles with different data access patterns

project_class.rationale: Requires Vue 3 web frontend for sales reps and warehouse staff plus Django REST API backend

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, admin_panel, integrations
capabilities.rationale: Full-stack with admin panel for catalog management and supplier API integration for replenishment

complexity_level.rationale: Standard CRUD with customer price tier logic and replenishment approval workflow; no real-time requirements

risk_level.rationale: Handles customer pricing and order history (financial data); migration risk from Access database with years of historical data

data_sensitivity.rationale: Customer pricing tiers and order history contain commercially sensitive financial data

external_exposure.rationale: Sales reps access remotely over HTTPS but all endpoints require JWT authentication

access_model.value: JWT-based RBAC with roles: sales_rep (own customers and catalog read), warehouse_staff (replenishment queue, inventory), purchasing_manager (replenishment approval, supplier management); Django admin for catalog management
access_model.rationale: Each role has distinct data access scope; sales reps should not see other customers' pricing

feature_scope.value: Product catalog with customer-specific pricing tier display, customer account management, stock level visibility, replenishment request creation and approval workflow, order history, supplier integration for purchase order submission, admin panel for catalog and pricing management
feature_scope.rationale: Complete replacement of all Access database functionality plus remote access and approval workflow

mvp_scope.value: Product catalog with pricing tier display, customer accounts, stock levels, replenishment request and approval workflow, historical data migrated from Access database
mvp_scope.rationale: Core sales and replenishment workflows needed to allow decommissioning Access database

security_baseline.value: HTTPS only, custom JWT with RS256 signing, RBAC enforced per endpoint, no SQL stored procedures with user input, parameterized queries only
security_baseline.rationale: Standard web application security for financial data with remote access

frontend_stack.rationale: Existing team Vue expertise; TypeScript for maintainability given complex pricing tier display logic

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Django 5 and Django REST Framework
backend_stack.rationale: Team Django expertise; DRF provides rapid API development; Django admin as free admin panel

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 with Redis 7 for session caching
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL for relational pricing tier and order data; Redis for JWT session invalidation blacklist

hosting_target.value: DigitalOcean App Platform with managed PostgreSQL
hosting_target.rationale: Simple managed hosting appropriate for distributor budget; DigitalOcean managed PostgreSQL handles backups and failover

privacy_retention_policy.value: Order history retained 7 years for tax and audit purposes; customer pricing agreements retained for contract duration plus 3 years; no personal data beyond business contact information
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: B2B distributor retention requirements for financial records

future_scope.value: Customer self-service order portal, EDI integration with major office supply manufacturers, mobile app for warehouse staff barcode scanning
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 after Access replacement stabilizes

constraints.value: Budget under $500/month hosting; must support 15 concurrent users; Access database data must be migrated without loss; 3-month delivery timeline
constraints.rationale: Distributor size and budget constraints

observability_baseline.value: New Relic APM for performance monitoring, structured Django logging to DigitalOcean log drain, email alerts on unhandled exceptions
observability_baseline.rationale: New Relic starter plan within budget; email alerts sufficient for small team operations

execution_preference.value: Iterative delivery with 2-week sprints; data migration validated in parallel with feature development; Access database remains live until parallel validation complete
execution_preference.rationale: Parallel run strategy reduces data migration risk

compliance_context.value: No formal regulatory compliance required; financial data sensitivity governed by customer pricing agreements (NDAs); standard B2B data protection practices apply
compliance_context.rationale: Regional B2B distributor with contractual rather than regulatory data obligations

project.goal: Replace Access database with modern cloud web app for product catalog, pricing, and replenishment
project.target_users: Sales reps, warehouse staff, purchasing manager

security.auth: Custom JWT RS256 with Redis blacklist

data.cache: Redis 7 for JWT blacklist and query caching
data.migration: Access database to PostgreSQL via custom Python migration script

open_questions.supplier_api: Supplier EDI vs REST API for purchase order submission TBD
open_questions.access_migration: Access .mdb file structure needs audit before migration script design

reasoner_reviews.product: The scope is well-defined: replace a specific Access database with equivalent functionality plus remote access. The pricing tier display is the most complex feature - sales reps need to see customer-specific prices across a 5000-SKU catalog without exposing other customers' pricing. The replenishment approval workflow is straightforward but needs email notification to purchasing manager on new requests.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Django 5 with DRF on DigitalOcean App Platform is well-matched to this scale: 15 concurrent users, $500/month budget, 3-month timeline. Django admin as the admin panel eliminates a significant build cost. The customer pricing tier model needs careful design: customers can be on standard, preferred, or contract pricing tiers with per-SKU override capability. A separate customer_price_overrides table for contract-specific prices is cleaner than embedding tier logic in the product table.
reasoner_reviews.security: Customer pricing data is commercially sensitive; a sales rep should only see pricing for their assigned customers. Django's ORM-level row filtering using the request user's assigned customer list is the correct enforcement point. JWT RS256 is appropriate; Redis blacklist handles logout invalidation. All Django queries must use parameterized ORM calls - no raw SQL with user input.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: DigitalOcean App Platform basic plan at $12/month per container plus managed PostgreSQL at $15/month comfortably fits the $500/month budget. 15 concurrent users is well within App Platform auto-scaling. 3-month timeline is achievable if data migration validation runs in parallel with feature development.

backend.service_design: Single Django 5 application with modular apps: catalog (products, categories, pricing), customers (accounts, pricing tiers, assignments), inventory (stock levels, replenishment requests), orders (order history), suppliers (purchase orders, contacts). DRF ViewSets for all API endpoints; Django admin registered for catalog and customer management.
backend.api_patterns: DRF ModelViewSets with router-generated URLs. Pricing endpoint applies customer tier at query time: GET /api/products/?customer_id=123 returns products with tier-adjusted prices. Replenishment workflow: POST /api/replenishment-requests (warehouse_staff), PATCH /api/replenishment-requests/:id/approve (purchasing_manager). Django signals trigger email notification on ReplenishmentRequest creation.
backend.business_modules: PricingEngine: resolves effective price for (sku, customer_id) by checking customer_price_overrides first, then customer tier rate from customer_price_tiers, then standard list price. ReplenishmentWorkflow: warehouse staff creates request with SKU, quantity, reason; system checks current stock_level; if stock < reorder_point, auto-suggests quantity; purchasing_manager receives email, approves or rejects, approved requests generate PurchaseOrder records.
backend.failure_handling: Django REST Framework exception handler for consistent JSON error responses. Database connection pool with pgbouncer on DigitalOcean. Retry logic on supplier API calls for purchase order submission (3 retries, exponential backoff).
backend.backend_risks: Access database migration: mdb file may have data quality issues (duplicate SKU codes, inconsistent pricing history). Migration script must validate and log all discrepancies before import. Supplier API format TBD; design purchase order submission as a pluggable adapter.

frontend.app_structure: Vue 3 SPA with Pinia state store and Vue Router. Modules: CatalogModule (product list, search, pricing display), CustomerModule (account detail, pricing history), InventoryModule (stock levels, replenishment request form), OrderHistoryModule, AdminLink (redirects to Django admin for purchasing_manager and admin roles).
frontend.pages_and_flows: Sales Rep flow: Login -> Customer selector -> Product catalog (prices resolved for selected customer) -> Submit replenishment flag. Warehouse Staff flow: Login -> Inventory dashboard (low-stock alerts) -> Create replenishment request -> Track approval status. Purchasing Manager flow: Login -> Replenishment approval queue -> Approve/Reject -> Generated PO list.
frontend.state_management: Pinia stores: AuthStore (JWT token, user role, assigned customers), CatalogStore (product list with tier pricing), InventoryStore (stock levels, replenishment queue). Axios interceptor attaches JWT Bearer header to all API calls; 401 response triggers redirect to login.
frontend.frontend_risks: Pricing tier display for 5000-SKU catalog requires pagination and server-side filtering; client-side rendering of full catalog is not feasible. Vue virtual scrolling with page size 50 and server-side search.

security.auth_design: Custom JWT RS256: Django generates tokens with user_id, role, assigned_customer_ids, exp (8-hour). Redis stores token JTI blacklist for logout invalidation. Refresh token (30-day) stored in HttpOnly cookie; access token (8-hour) in memory (not localStorage).
security.authorization_model: Django permission groups: sales_rep group allows read on catalog, customers (own assignments only), inventory (read); warehouse_staff allows create on replenishment_requests, read on inventory; purchasing_manager allows approve/reject on replenishment_requests, read all. Row-level enforcement: CatalogViewSet filters QuerySet by request.user.assigned_customers for sales_rep role.
security.secrets_management: DigitalOcean App Platform environment variables (encrypted at rest) for JWT private key, database URL, Redis URL, New Relic license key. No secrets in codebase or Docker images.
security.security_risks: Pricing tier data in JWT payload would expose competitor pricing if token intercepted; pricing is resolved server-side per request, not embedded in token.

data.entities: product_catalog (id UUID PK, sku VARCHAR UNIQUE, name, category_id FK, list_price DECIMAL, unit_of_measure, reorder_point INT, active BOOL, created_at). customer_price_tiers (id UUID PK, tier_name ENUM standard/preferred/contract, sku_id FK, tier_price DECIMAL, effective_from DATE, effective_to DATE). customer_accounts (id UUID PK, company_name, assigned_tier ENUM, assigned_rep_id FK, credit_limit DECIMAL, created_at). customer_price_overrides (id UUID PK, customer_id FK, sku_id FK, override_price DECIMAL, contract_ref VARCHAR, valid_from DATE, valid_to DATE). stock_levels (id UUID PK, sku_id FK UNIQUE, on_hand_qty INT, reserved_qty INT, last_counted_at). replenishment_requests (id UUID PK, sku_id FK, requested_qty INT, current_stock INT, reason TEXT, status ENUM pending/approved/rejected/ordered, created_by FK, reviewed_by FK, created_at, reviewed_at). order_history (id UUID PK, customer_id FK, sku_id FK, quantity INT, unit_price DECIMAL, order_date DATE, source ENUM access_import/new_order). sales_rep_accounts (id UUID PK, user_id FK, customer_ids UUID[]).
data.migration_notes: Access .mdb tables: tblProducts -> product_catalog + stock_levels. tblCustomers -> customer_accounts. tblPricing -> customer_price_tiers + customer_price_overrides. tblOrders -> order_history (source='access_import'). Migration script in Python using pyodbc to read Access tables, data cleaning pass (deduplication, price validation), bulk INSERT to PostgreSQL via psycopg3 COPY. Parallel validation: Access and PostgreSQL outputs compared for 30 days before Access decommission.
data.data_risks: Access database may contain pricing records without customer assignments (orphaned tier rows). Migration script must flag and route these to a review queue before import. SKU duplicates in Access tblProducts require deduplication logic with stakeholder review.

devops.deployment_topology: DigitalOcean App Platform: one web service (Django + gunicorn, 1 container, auto-scale to 3), one worker service (Celery for email notifications and async purchase order submission, 1 container). Managed PostgreSQL 16 (1 vCPU, 1GB, daily backups). Managed Redis (1GB). DigitalOcean Spaces for static file serving (Django collectstatic).
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: pytest (90% coverage for PricingEngine and ReplenishmentWorkflow) -> mypy -> ruff -> Docker build -> App Platform deploy (auto-deploy on main merge for staging; manual deploy for production). Database migrations via django-admin migrate run as App Platform deploy job.
devops.environments: Dev: local Docker Compose (PostgreSQL + Redis). Staging: App Platform with production-size data subset (anonymized customer pricing). Production: App Platform with DigitalOcean managed PostgreSQL and Redis.
devops.devops_risks: DigitalOcean App Platform does not support zero-downtime Django migration for table-locking ALTER TABLE commands. Use django-safemigrate or review each migration for lock implications before production deployment.

target_output.thinking_summary: First-pass architecture for a Django+Vue 3 replacement of a legacy Access database. Key decisions: PricingEngine resolves effective price server-side using a three-level hierarchy (customer_price_overrides > customer_price_tiers > list_price) to prevent pricing data from leaking into JWT tokens or frontend state. Django admin serves as the free admin panel. Data migration runs in parallel with feature development for 30 days with Access as the authoritative source. DigitalOcean App Platform stays comfortably within $500/month budget.
target_output.title: Office Supplies Distributor Platform - Legacy Access Migration
target_output.executive_summary: A Vue 3 and Django 5 fullstack web application replacing a regional office supplies distributor's legacy Microsoft Access database with a cloud-hosted system accessible by sales reps, warehouse staff, and purchasing managers. Core features include a 5000-SKU product catalog with customer-specific pricing tier display, replenishment request and approval workflow with email notification, historical data migration from Access, and Django admin for catalog management. Deployed to DigitalOcean App Platform with managed PostgreSQL within a $500/month budget.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-tier architecture: Vue 3 SPA served from DigitalOcean Spaces via CDN, communicating over HTTPS to a Django 5 DRF REST API on DigitalOcean App Platform, backed by managed PostgreSQL 16 and Redis 7. Authentication via custom JWT RS256 with Redis-backed token blacklist for logout invalidation. PricingEngine resolves customer-specific prices server-side per request: checks customer_price_overrides (contract pricing), then customer_price_tiers (tier rate), then product list_price. Row-level filtering in Django QuerySets ensures sales reps only see pricing for their assigned customers. Celery worker handles async email notifications to purchasing manager on new replenishment requests and purchase order submission to supplier API. Data migration from Access .mdb runs in parallel using pyodbc reader and PostgreSQL COPY bulk insert, with 30-day parallel validation before Access decommission.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Vue 3 with TypeScript, Pinia state management, Vuetify 3 component library, Vue Router 4, Axios with JWT interceptor. Backend: Python 3.12, Django 5, Django REST Framework 3.15, Celery 5 (async tasks), Channels not needed (no WebSocket). Data: PostgreSQL 16 (DigitalOcean managed), Redis 7 (DigitalOcean managed, JWT blacklist and query cache). Migration: pyodbc for Access .mdb reading, psycopg3 for PostgreSQL COPY. Auth: custom JWT RS256, python-jose. Infra: DigitalOcean App Platform, Spaces for static files. Monitoring: New Relic APM Python agent.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Product Catalog: paginated SKU listing (50 per page) with server-side search by SKU, name, category; prices resolved per-request for authenticated customer context; sales_rep sees only authorized customer pricing. Customer Pricing Management (Django admin): create/update customer_price_tiers and customer_price_overrides per contract; assign customers to sales reps. Inventory Dashboard: stock_levels with on_hand vs reorder_point alerts; warehouse_staff sees all, sales_rep sees summary. Replenishment Workflow: warehouse_staff creates request with SKU, quantity, reason; Celery sends email to purchasing_manager distribution; purchasing_manager reviews queue, approves or rejects; approved requests generate purchase_orders. Order History: filterable by customer, date range, SKU; migrated Access records marked as source='access_import'. Admin Panel: Django admin for product_catalog, customer_accounts, pricing tier management, user account creation.
target_output.system_components: DigitalOcean App Platform web service: Django 5 gunicorn (1 container, auto-scale to 3 on CPU >70%). App Platform worker service: Celery (1 container, processes email-notification and purchase-order-submit queues). DigitalOcean Managed PostgreSQL 16 (1 vCPU, 1GB RAM, automated daily backups, point-in-time recovery). DigitalOcean Managed Redis 7 (1GB, JWT blacklist and DRF query caching). DigitalOcean Spaces: Django static files and uploaded documents served via CDN. GitHub Actions: CI/CD pipeline. New Relic APM: Django middleware agent for transaction traces and error tracking.
target_output.workflows: Sales Rep Catalog Pricing Workflow: Sales rep selects a customer account from their assigned list. Frontend sends GET /api/products/?customer_id=:id&search=:query&page=:n. PricingEngine for each product: SELECT from customer_price_overrides WHERE customer_id=:id AND sku_id=:sku AND now() BETWEEN valid_from AND valid_to (contract override); if not found, SELECT from customer_price_tiers WHERE tier=customer.assigned_tier AND sku_id=:sku (tier price); if not found, product.list_price. Response includes resolved_price per product without exposing other tier prices. Replenishment Request and Approval Workflow: Warehouse staff submits POST /api/replenishment-requests/ with sku_id, requested_qty, reason. Django view creates ReplenishmentRequest (status=pending), fires Django signal. Celery task sends email to all users in purchasing_manager group with request detail and link to approval queue. Purchasing manager opens GET /api/replenishment-requests/?status=pending, reviews request, calls PATCH /api/replenishment-requests/:id/approve or /reject with notes. Approved: Django creates PurchaseOrder record, queues Celery task to POST to supplier REST API (adapter pattern, fallback to PDF email if API unavailable).
target_output.data_model: product_catalog (id UUID PK, sku VARCHAR(50) UNIQUE NOT NULL, name VARCHAR(200), category_id FK, list_price DECIMAL(10,2), unit_of_measure VARCHAR(20), reorder_point INT DEFAULT 10, active BOOL DEFAULT TRUE, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). customer_price_tiers (id UUID PK, tier_name ENUM standard/preferred/contract, sku_id FK, tier_price DECIMAL(10,2), effective_from DATE, effective_to DATE NULLABLE). customer_accounts (id UUID PK, company_name VARCHAR(200), assigned_tier ENUM standard/preferred/contract, assigned_rep_id FK NULLABLE, credit_limit DECIMAL(12,2), tax_id VARCHAR(20), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). customer_price_overrides (id UUID PK, customer_id FK, sku_id FK, override_price DECIMAL(10,2), contract_ref VARCHAR(100), valid_from DATE, valid_to DATE). stock_levels (id UUID PK, sku_id FK UNIQUE, on_hand_qty INT DEFAULT 0, reserved_qty INT DEFAULT 0, last_counted_at TIMESTAMPTZ). replenishment_requests (id UUID PK, sku_id FK, requested_qty INT, current_stock INT, reason TEXT, status ENUM pending/approved/rejected/ordered DEFAULT pending, created_by FK, reviewed_by FK NULLABLE, reviewed_at TIMESTAMPTZ NULLABLE, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). order_history (id UUID PK, customer_id FK, sku_id FK, quantity INT, unit_price DECIMAL(10,2), order_date DATE, source ENUM access_import/new_order DEFAULT new_order). sales_rep_accounts (id UUID PK, user_id FK UNIQUE, assigned_customer_ids UUID[] DEFAULT '{}').
target_output.api_design: GET /api/products/?customer_id=&search=&category=&page= (sales_rep, warehouse_staff) - returns paginated products with resolved_price for customer context. GET /api/products/:sku/ (all auth roles). GET /api/customers/ (sales_rep sees own assignments only, purchasing_manager sees all). GET /api/customers/:id/pricing-history/ (sales_rep for own customers, purchasing_manager). GET /api/stock-levels/?low_stock_only=true (warehouse_staff, purchasing_manager). POST /api/replenishment-requests/ (warehouse_staff) - body: {sku_id, requested_qty, reason}. GET /api/replenishment-requests/?status= (purchasing_manager sees all, warehouse_staff sees own). PATCH /api/replenishment-requests/:id/approve (purchasing_manager) - body: {notes}. PATCH /api/replenishment-requests/:id/reject (purchasing_manager) - body: {reason}. GET /api/order-history/?customer_id=&from_date=&to_date= (sales_rep for own customers). POST /api/auth/login, POST /api/auth/refresh, POST /api/auth/logout. All endpoints require JWT Bearer; DRF permission classes enforce role.
target_output.security_and_compliance: JWT RS256: private key in DigitalOcean App Platform encrypted environment variables; 8-hour access token lifetime; 30-day refresh token in HttpOnly Secure SameSite=Strict cookie; JTI stored in Redis blacklist on logout with TTL matching token expiry. Row-level security: CatalogViewSet.get_queryset() checks request.user.salesrepaccount.assigned_customer_ids for sales_rep role; purchasing_manager and warehouse_staff bypass to full QuerySet. Parameterized queries enforced via Django ORM; no raw SQL. HTTPS enforced: App Platform provides managed TLS termination; HSTS header set. No customer pricing data in JWT payload; pricing resolved per request. Django admin accessible only to admin and purchasing_manager roles; protected by CSRF and session auth separate from API JWT. Rate limiting: django-ratelimit on login endpoint (5 attempts per minute per IP).
target_output.deployment_and_operations: DigitalOcean App Platform spec: web service (Python, gunicorn, 1 instance, auto-scale to 3 at CPU 70%, 512MB RAM), worker service (Celery, 1 instance, 256MB RAM). GitHub Actions pipeline: ruff lint -> mypy strict -> pytest (90% coverage gate for PricingEngine + ReplenishmentWorkflow) -> Docker build -> App Platform deploy via doctl API. Staging auto-deploys on main merge; production requires manual workflow_dispatch approval. Django migrations run as App Platform deploy job (pre-deploy command: python manage.py migrate --run-syncdb). Static files: python manage.py collectstatic to DigitalOcean Spaces. Environments: dev (Docker Compose with PostgreSQL + Redis + MailHog for email testing), staging (App Platform with anonymized copy of Access-migrated data), production. Secrets managed in App Platform encrypted environment variables; no .env files in repository.
target_output.observability: New Relic APM Python agent: transaction traces for PricingEngine query performance (target p95 < 200ms for catalog page with 50 products), error tracking for unhandled exceptions. Structured Django logging to DigitalOcean log drain: request_id, user_id, action, duration_ms, status_code. Celery task monitoring via Flower dashboard (internal, not public). Alerts: New Relic email alert on error rate >1% for 5 minutes; App Platform sends downtime email; Celery email-notification queue depth alert if >50 pending tasks.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ~$85/month estimated: App Platform web service ($12), worker service ($12), managed PostgreSQL Basic ($15), managed Redis ($15), Spaces storage + CDN ($5), New Relic Lite ($0), GitHub Actions (free tier). Total ~$59/month, well within $500/month budget with headroom for growth. Scales to 50 concurrent users with auto-scale to 3 web instances; managed PostgreSQL can be vertically scaled when needed.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (6 weeks): Django project structure, all data models, PricingEngine with three-tier resolution, product catalog API and Vue catalog page, customer account management, Access .mdb migration script development and dry-run against staging, JWT auth with RBAC, Django admin for catalog. Phase 2 (4 weeks): Replenishment request and approval workflow, Celery email notifications, order history API and Vue page, full Access data migration to staging with parallel validation, purchase order record creation. Phase 3 (2 weeks): Production cutover - parallel run for 30 days with Access, validate pricing and order history parity, decommission Access, supplier API adapter for purchase order submission.
target_output.development_guidelines: Django apps split by domain: catalog, customers, inventory, orders, suppliers. DRF serializers for all API input/output; no direct model-to-JSON serialization. PricingEngine extracted as a pure Python class in catalog/pricing.py with 100% unit test coverage (parameterized tests for each pricing tier scenario). mypy strict mode in CI. ruff for linting. All QuerySets in ViewSets must call .select_related() on FK fields to avoid N+1 queries on catalog list. Migration scripts in a separate migrations_access/ directory with integration tests against a sample Access .mdb fixture. Django admin customizations in admin.py per app; no admin logic in models.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Access .mdb data quality issues (duplicate SKUs, orphaned pricing rows) discovered mid-migration. Mitigation: migration script runs validation pass before INSERT, outputs discrepancy report for stakeholder review; migration blocked until all errors resolved or explicitly accepted. Risk: customer_price_overrides table has overlapping date ranges for same customer/SKU from Access data. Mitigation: migration script detects overlaps and flags for manual review; PricingEngine uses most-recently-effective row as tiebreaker. Tradeoff: custom JWT over Django session auth provides stateless API suitable for future mobile app, at the cost of Redis blacklist dependency for logout. Tradeoff: Django admin as admin panel saves 2 weeks of development vs custom Vue admin views, but limits admin UX customization in Phase 1.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed DigitalOcean App Platform over DigitalOcean Droplets for managed deployment without Nginx/gunicorn configuration overhead. Selected Vuetify 3 over Tailwind for Vue UI to accelerate development with prebuilt data table and form components matching the distributor's expected desktop-first workflow. Access migration will read .mdb via pyodbc on Windows VM provided by the distributor; PostgreSQL COPY is the import path.

metadata.notes: First-pass migration from legacy Access database to Django+Vue 3 for a regional office supplies distributor; moderate quality with slightly thin supplier integration section.

project_goal.value: CLI tool to migrate product and inventory data from Magento 1.x stores to a modern inventory management platform with field mapping, batch validation, checkpoint tracking, and rollback on errors
project_goal.rationale: Core need to decommission Magento 1.x before EOL without losing catalog data
