previous_plan.title: Assortment Optimization Platform - Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: React 18 and FastAPI web app on ECS Fargate surfacing weekly ML-scored SKU assortment recommendations with accept/override workflow and GPT-4o explanations.
previous_plan.llm_integration_design: GPT-4o called with full competitor_prices rows for context; response cached in Redis 7 days.
previous_plan.background_jobs: Single ECS Fargate task runs full weekly pipeline; no stage checkpointing.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Cognito MFA, category-scoped RBAC, TLS 1.3. LLM prompt security not addressed.

best_plan.title: Assortment Optimization Platform - Round 1
best_plan.executive_summary: React 18 and FastAPI web app on ECS Fargate surfacing weekly ML-scored SKU assortment recommendations with accept/override workflow and GPT-4o explanations.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 addresses two issues from the audit: ISS-001 (LLM prompt exposes raw competitor prices) fixed by replacing raw prices with derived signals in a sanitized prompt schema; ISS-002 (no ML pipeline checkpoint) fixed by designing a checkpoint-resumable DAG with pipeline_stage_checkpoints table and S3 intermediate artifacts per stage. Both fixes require changes to the llm_integration_design and background_jobs sections.

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced raw competitor_prices rows in LLM prompt with a sanitized PromptSignals object containing only derived fields: price_gap_percentile (0-100 integer), competitive_rank_bucket ('top_quartile'/'mid'/'bottom_quartile'), trend_direction ('gaining'/'losing'/'stable'). Added PromptSanitizer class with schema validation before any OpenAI API call.
fix_report.expected_outcome: No raw competitor price values reach OpenAI; SOC 2 audit can demonstrate data minimization in LLM interactions; legal risk from competitive data leakage eliminated

fix_report.action_taken: Redesigned ML pipeline as a 5-stage DAG tracked in pipeline_stage_checkpoints table. Each stage writes intermediate artifacts to S3 (features_v{run_id}.parquet, scores_v{run_id}.parquet, shap_v{run_id}.parquet). A coordinator Lambda function checks checkpoint table on startup and resumes from the last completed stage rather than restarting.
fix_report.expected_outcome: A pipeline failure at any stage resumes from the last completed checkpoint on retry; Sunday 4-hour window risk eliminated for partial failures

target_output.title: Assortment Optimization Platform
target_output.executive_summary: A React 18 and FastAPI web application on AWS ECS Fargate for retail category managers, surfacing weekly ML-scored SKU assortment recommendations (add, discontinue, reposition) with accept/override decision capture, SHAP-backed model explanations, and GPT-4o natural-language summaries. A checkpoint-resumable 5-stage ML pipeline runs Sunday nights within a 4-hour window. Cognito RBAC with category-scoped data access protects confidential competitive pricing intelligence.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-tier architecture: React 18 SPA served via CloudFront communicating with a FastAPI REST API on ECS Fargate behind an ALB. PostgreSQL 16 on RDS Multi-AZ stores assortment decisions, recommendation results, and audit log. Redis 7 (ElastiCache) caches recommendation lists (1-hour TTL) and LLM explanations (7-day TTL). A separate ECS Fargate task definition runs the weekly ML pipeline as a checkpoint-resumable 5-stage DAG triggered by EventBridge; each stage writes S3 artifacts and updates a pipeline_stage_checkpoints table enabling resume on failure. GPT-4o explanation calls use a PromptSanitizer that replaces raw competitor prices with derived percentile signals before dispatch.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Vite, TanStack Query, TanStack Virtual, Zustand, Recharts. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async, Alembic, pydantic v2. ML: scikit-learn, SHAP, pandas, pyarrow. LLM: OpenAI GPT-4o via openai-python SDK with PromptSanitizer. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ, ElastiCache Redis 7, S3 for ML artifacts, EventBridge, CloudFront, ALB, Cognito. CI/CD: GitHub Actions, ECR, New Relic APM.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Recommendation Feed: paginated list per assigned category, filterable by recommendation_type (add/discontinue/reposition) and confidence band; each row shows SKU name, category, confidence score, key demand/margin signals. SKU Detail Panel: demand trend chart (Recharts), margin history chart, competitor gap level indicator, SHAP waterfall chart showing feature contributions, GPT-4o natural-language explanation. Accept/Override Workflow: one-click accept or override dialog with structured reason enum (price_too_high, wrong_category, supplier_issue, seasonal_exception, other) plus free-text; decision feeds back to model_feedback table. Analytics Dashboard: category-level accept rate, override rate by reason type, margin impact of accepted recommendations. Admin Panel: category assignments per manager, model version info, manual pipeline retraining trigger.
target_output.system_components: CloudFront distribution for React SPA assets. ALB with Cognito authorizer routing to ECS Fargate API cluster (2-4 tasks, HPA). RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ with read replica for analytics queries. ElastiCache Redis 7 for recommendation and explanation caching. S3 bucket for ML pipeline stage artifacts (features, scores, SHAP values partitioned by pipeline_run_id). EventBridge weekly rule triggering ML pipeline ECS task. Lambda coordinator function managing pipeline stage sequencing and checkpoint resume logic. Secrets Manager for OpenAI API key with 30-day rotation. New Relic APM agent sidecar on API tasks.
target_output.workflows: Category Manager Decision Workflow: Manager logs in, Cognito JWT with category_assignments claim issued; recommendation feed loads top 20 pending recommendations for assigned categories from Redis cache (falls back to PostgreSQL); manager opens SKU detail, explanation fetched from Redis or GPT-4o generated; manager accepts (status -> accepted, audit_log entry written) or opens override dialog, selects reason enum, adds note, submits (status -> overridden, reason stored in model_feedback); weekly model retrain incorporates override feedback. ML Pipeline Execution (Weekly Sunday): EventBridge triggers coordinator Lambda at 2 AM; coordinator checks pipeline_stage_checkpoints for prior incomplete run and resumes from last completed stage or starts stage 1 (feature extraction from PostgreSQL to S3 parquet); stage 2 scikit-learn scoring per SKU writing scores parquet to S3; stage 3 SHAP computation for top 500 recommendations; stage 4 LLM explanation batch for top 200 using PromptSanitizer; stage 5 bulk load to optimization_recommendations table and Redis cache invalidation; coordinator marks pipeline_run complete.
target_output.data_model: product_assortments (sku_id VARCHAR PK, category_id FK, current_status ENUM active/discontinued/trial, margin_pct NUMERIC, demand_percentile INT, last_scored_at TIMESTAMPTZ). optimization_recommendations (id UUID PK, sku_id FK, category_id FK, recommendation_type ENUM add/discontinue/reposition, confidence_score NUMERIC, demand_signal_json JSONB, margin_analysis_json JSONB, competitor_gap_json JSONB -- contains gap_percentile and rank_bucket not raw prices, shap_values_json JSONB, manager_decision ENUM pending/accepted/overridden, decision_reason_enum VARCHAR, decision_reason_text TEXT, decided_by UUID, decided_at TIMESTAMPTZ, pipeline_run_id UUID FK). model_explanations (recommendation_id UUID PK FK, explanation_text TEXT, prompt_signals_json JSONB, generated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, model_version VARCHAR). pipeline_stage_checkpoints (run_id UUID, stage_name VARCHAR, status ENUM pending/running/completed/failed, artifact_s3_key VARCHAR, started_at, completed_at, PRIMARY KEY (run_id, stage_name)). model_feedback (id UUID PK, recommendation_id FK, decision ENUM accepted/overridden, reason_enum VARCHAR, reason_text TEXT, category_id, created_at). audit_log (id UUID PK, user_id FK, recommendation_id FK, action VARCHAR, category_id, metadata_json JSONB, created_at) -- append-only.
target_output.api_design: REST at /api/v1. GET /recommendations?category_id=&type=&status=pending (paginated, category-scoped from JWT claim). GET /recommendations/:id (full SKU detail with signals). GET /recommendations/:id/explanation (LLM explanation, Redis cache check first, generates if miss). PATCH /recommendations/:id/decision body {decision: 'accepted'|'overridden', reason_enum, reason_text}. GET /analytics/category/:id/summary (accept rate, override breakdown, margin impact). GET /admin/categories (list with assignment counts). PUT /admin/categories/:id/assignments (update manager assignments). POST /admin/pipeline/trigger (admin only, manual retraining trigger). GET /admin/pipeline/status (latest run checkpoint status).
target_output.security_and_compliance: Cognito user pool with mandatory MFA. ALB Cognito authorizer validates JWT before requests reach FastAPI; FastAPI middleware extracts category_assignments claim for downstream authorization. Category-scoped queries: all recommendation endpoints filter by category_id IN (jwt.category_assignments) -- director role receives wildcard. PromptSanitizer class validates GPT-4o prompt schema before API call; raises PromptSanitizationError if any raw price fields detected; only PromptSignals object (price_gap_percentile INT, competitive_rank_bucket ENUM, trend_direction ENUM) permitted in prompts. SOC 2 Type II: audit_log captures every recommendation decision with user identity and category; CloudWatch log retention 5 years; Secrets Manager for OpenAI key with 30-day rotation. TLS 1.3 at ALB.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: ruff + mypy strict, pytest 80% coverage gate, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy to staging with smoke tests (recommendation feed loads, decision PATCH returns 200, explanation generates), manual approval gate for production. ECS Fargate API service: 2-4 tasks with ALB target tracking CPU 60%. ML pipeline: ECS task definition with 4 vCPU / 16GB RAM for SHAP computation; EventBridge weekly trigger Sunday 2 AM; coordinator Lambda sequences stages and handles resume logic. RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ with automated daily backups and point-in-time recovery. Alembic migrations run as ECS one-off task pre-deployment. ElastiCache Redis with in-transit encryption and daily snapshots.
target_output.observability: New Relic APM traces on FastAPI: recommendation_feed_p95_ms, explanation_generate_duration_ms, llm_explanation_cache_hit_rate, decision_submit_duration_ms. ML pipeline metrics in CloudWatch: pipeline_stage_duration_seconds per stage_name, pipeline_total_duration_minutes, sku_scored_count, explanation_generated_count, llm_error_rate. Business metrics logged to structured CloudWatch namespace: override_rate_by_category_and_reason (weekly), recommendation_acceptance_rate_by_confidence_band. PagerDuty alerts: ML pipeline stage failure, recommendation feed load p95 > 3s, LLM error rate > 10%.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $1600-1900/month: RDS Multi-AZ ~$350, ElastiCache ~$90, ECS Fargate API ~$200, ML pipeline task ~$50/week, CloudFront ~$30, OpenAI GPT-4o for ~200 explanations/week ~$15. At 200K SKU scale: partition optimization_recommendations by category_id and add read replica for analytics queries; migrate ML pipeline from single ECS task to Step Functions parallel category processing.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks, 3 pilot categories): Cognito RBAC, recommendation feed with accept/override, model explanation display, 5-stage checkpoint pipeline with feature extraction + scoring, basic PromptSanitizer, PostgreSQL schema, ECS Fargate deployment, GitHub Actions CI. Phase 2 (6 weeks, full category rollout): SHAP waterfall charts, LLM explanation batch with Redis caching, override feedback integration into model retraining, analytics dashboard with accept/override rates, admin panel. Phase 3 (8 weeks): A/B assortment experiment framework, competitor price trend charts, mobile-responsive director dashboard, model version comparison view.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with mypy strict and ruff. FastAPI dependency injection for Cognito JWT parsing and category scope enforcement. PromptSanitizer is tested independently with a suite of prompt objects including adversarial cases with raw price fields -- must reject any prompt not matching PromptSignals schema. All ML pipeline stages must write to S3 artifact key before marking checkpoint as completed. 80% test coverage gate. ADRs for: PromptSanitizer design, checkpoint resume pattern, SHAP vs LIME selection for explanations.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: GPT-4o prompt injection via free-text override reasons fed back into future explanation context. Mitigation: override reasons stored separately and never included in LLM prompts; only PromptSignals objects used. Risk: Sunday ML pipeline window overrun at high SKU volume. Mitigation: checkpoint resume eliminates full restarts; parallel category processing in Phase 3 reduces single-task duration. Tradeoff: FastAPI + SQLAlchemy async over Django ORM -- better async performance for concurrent recommendation fetches at the cost of more explicit query management. Tradeoff: scikit-learn over deep learning for scoring -- interpretable SHAP values are critical for manager trust; neural model accuracy gains not worth losing explainability.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: PromptSanitizer design selected over per-field redaction to provide schema-validated safety boundary. S3 parquet intermediate artifacts chosen for pipeline checkpointing over database-only storage to keep large feature matrices out of PostgreSQL. TanStack Virtual selected over react-window for recommendation list virtualization due to better TanStack Query integration. Override reason stored as enum plus free text to enable both model feedback aggregation and qualitative analysis.

metadata.notes: Round 2 fixes LLM prompt data leakage (ISS-001) and ML pipeline checkpoint gap (ISS-002) for retail assortment optimization app.

profile.domain: Consumer Electronics Retail - Product Recall & Warranty Compliance

project_goal.value: Web application for a consumer electronics retailer to manage product recall and warranty compliance tracking, flagging recalled products across inventory, sending legally compliant customer notifications, and generating CPSC-formatted regulatory reports within required timeframes
project_goal.rationale: Current process is manual spreadsheets; a recalled product entering service without customer notification creates regulatory and legal liability

target_users.value: Compliance officers, customer service managers, and legal team administrators
target_users.rationale: Primary users managing recall scope, notification approvals, and regulatory submissions

project_class.rationale: Browser-based compliance workflow tool for internal operations staff

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, integrations, admin_panel, batch_jobs
capabilities.rationale: Full-stack app with batch notification jobs, ERP/inventory system integration, and admin panel for compliance configuration

complexity_level.rationale: CPSC report generation format, batch notification workflows, and regulatory deadline tracking add meaningful complexity beyond CRUD

risk_level.rationale: Customer PII in warranty registrations, regulatory reporting obligations, and legal exposure from notification failures require high risk classification

data_sensitivity.rationale: Customer names, emails, phone numbers, and purchase addresses in warranty registrations constitute personal data under CCPA and GDPR

external_exposure.rationale: Internal compliance tool; all users authenticate via Azure AD B2C

access_model.value: RBAC: compliance_officer (create recalls, approve notifications, generate CPSC reports), cs_manager (view recall status, customer notification status), legal_admin (full access including regulatory submission history), readonly (audit view only)
access_model.rationale: Legal and compliance roles require different action permissions to enforce approval gating

feature_scope.value: Recall record creation with affected product model/date range, automatic inventory and warranty lookup to identify affected units and customers, notification batch management with legal template approval, CPSC Form 3554-compliant report generation, compliance deadline tracking dashboard, remediation action tracking (refund/replacement/repair)
feature_scope.rationale: End-to-end recall management from incident creation to regulatory reporting

mvp_scope.value: Recall creation, affected inventory/customer identification, notification batch dispatch via email and SMS, basic CPSC report PDF export
mvp_scope.rationale: Core regulatory compliance workflow validated first

security_baseline.value: Azure AD B2C with MFA, TLS 1.3, AES-256 at rest, PII fields encrypted at column level, access logs for all PII access events
security_baseline.rationale: CCPA and GDPR require PII protection; regulatory compliance requires audit trail

frontend_stack.rationale: SSR for compliance dashboard; team expertise in Next.js

backend_stack.value: Node.js 22 with Express
backend_stack.rationale: Existing team expertise; Express ecosystem for regulatory PDF generation libraries

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16
data_platform.rationale: Relational model for recall records, warranty registrations, and notification batches with ACID guarantees for regulatory data

hosting_target.value: Azure App Service
hosting_target.rationale: Existing Azure enterprise agreement; App Service for simplified Node.js deployment without container orchestration overhead

privacy_retention_policy.value: Recall records and CPSC reports retained indefinitely per legal mandate; customer notification records retained 10 years; warranty registration PII purged 7 years after warranty expiry per CCPA retention limits
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: CPSC regulations require indefinite recall record retention; CCPA limits unnecessary PII retention

future_scope.value: Automated CPSC electronic filing via CPSC Recalls Portal API, multi-channel notification (push, postal mail), supplier recall cost recovery tracking
future_scope.rationale: Roadmap items after core compliance workflow is proven

constraints.value: Must integrate with existing inventory ERP via REST API and customer database via read-only SQL view; CPSC reports must be generated within 15 minutes of request; budget $800/month Azure
constraints.rationale: ERP and customer DB are existing systems; CPSC filing deadlines are legally mandated; budget constraint favors App Service over AKS

observability_baseline.value: Azure Monitor with Application Insights, structured JSON logging, email alerts for batch notification failures and CPSC report generation errors
observability_baseline.rationale: Azure-native monitoring aligns with existing corporate standard

execution_preference.value: Waterfall MVP then agile sprints; compliance features must be spec-reviewed by legal before implementation
execution_preference.rationale: Legal sign-off on notification templates and CPSC report format before any code ships

compliance_context.value: CPSC recall reporting regulations (Consumer Product Safety Act); CCPA (California customer data); GDPR (EU customer warranty registrations); data retention per legal counsel guidance
compliance_context.rationale: Multi-jurisdiction personal data and federal regulatory reporting obligations

project.goal: End-to-end product recall management from incident creation to CPSC regulatory reporting with customer notification dispatch
project.target_users: Compliance officers, CS managers, legal administrators
project.timeline: MVP in 14 weeks with legal sign-off checkpoints

frontend.state: React Query for server state, local state for notification batch drafts

backend.notifications: Azure Communication Services for email and SMS

security.encryption: Column-level encryption for customer PII fields
security.access_logs: All PII access logged to compliance_audit_log

data.cache: None -- compliance data must always be current

reasoner_reviews.product: The compliance deadline tracker is the feature with the highest operational risk value. CPSC requires initial recall notification within 24 hours and reporting within 15 business days. The system must surface these deadlines prominently and send internal alerts well before they expire -- not just display them. Build an internal notification system (email to compliance_officer role) that fires at 72 hours, 48 hours, and 24 hours before each CPSC filing deadline.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The affected customer identification query is the performance-critical path: joining product_recalls against warranty_registrations on model number and purchase date range could scan millions of rows for popular product lines. Design this as a background batch job triggered on recall creation rather than a synchronous API call, with results loaded into affected_customers table and a status webhook to the frontend.
reasoner_reviews.security: Column-level encryption for customer PII (name, email, phone, address) is mandatory given CCPA and GDPR. Use pgcrypto symmetric encryption with a key stored in Azure Key Vault. The compliance_audit_log must record every access to decrypted PII fields -- not just write events. The notification batch approval workflow must require compliance_officer role sign-off before any customer data is used in outbound messages.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 15-minute CPSC report generation SLA is achievable with pre-aggregated recall summary data but requires that the notification delivery status be continuously updated in the background. Do not block report generation on live notification status fetching from Azure Communication Services; instead poll delivery status asynchronously and reflect it in the report as 'as of [timestamp]'.

backend.service_design: Express TypeScript server with domain modules: recalls (create, scope, status), affected_lookup (async identification batch), notifications (batch management, approval, dispatch), reports (CPSC PDF generation), compliance (deadline tracking, internal alerts)
backend.api_patterns: REST at /api/v1; recall creation returns recall_id and triggers async affected lookup job; GET /recalls/:id/affected-customers returns paginated results as they populate; POST /notifications/batches creates draft for approval; POST /notifications/batches/:id/dispatch sends approved batch
backend.background_jobs: Affected customer identification: triggered by recall creation, queries warranty_registrations and ERP inventory API, writes to affected_customers and affected_inventory tables, emits progress events via Server-Sent Events. Notification dispatch: processes approved notification batches in chunks of 500 via Azure Communication Services, tracks per-recipient delivery status. Compliance deadline alerts: daily cron checks cpsc_reports.deadline_date, sends email alerts to compliance_officer role at 72h/48h/24h thresholds.
backend.failure_handling: Notification dispatch failures logged per-recipient to notification_delivery_log with retry_at; failed deliveries retried up to 3 times with exponential backoff; undeliverable recipients flagged for manual follow-up in UI

frontend.app_structure: Next.js SSR pages for recall list and compliance deadline dashboard; client-side React for recall creation wizard and notification batch management
frontend.pages_and_flows: Recall Dashboard (active recalls, compliance deadline countdown, affected unit counts), Recall Detail (affected inventory, affected customers, notification status, CPSC report status), Notification Batch Manager (draft, legal template selection, approve, dispatch, delivery status), CPSC Report Viewer (PDF preview, generation, submission status), Admin Panel (legal templates, notification settings, user RBAC)
frontend.state_management: React Query for recall status and notification delivery polling; Server-Sent Events subscription for affected lookup progress updates
frontend.performance_notes: Affected customer list uses cursor-based pagination for large datasets; notification delivery status table virtualized for batches >1000 recipients

security.auth_design: Azure AD B2C OIDC integration; Next.js middleware validates session token; Express JWT middleware validates bearer token on API routes
security.authorization_model: RBAC enforced in Express middleware: compliance_officer (recall CRUD, notification approval, CPSC report generation), cs_manager (recall and notification status view only), legal_admin (all access + CPSC submission history), readonly (dashboard view only)
security.audit_and_logging_controls: compliance_audit_log table records every PII field decryption event (user_id, customer_id, fields_accessed, purpose, timestamp); separate recall_action_log records every state change (recall status, notification approval, report generation) for CPSC evidence

data.entities: product_recalls (id, recall_number, product_model, product_description, hazard_description, affected_date_range_start, affected_date_range_end, status ENUM draft/active/closed, cpsc_submission_status, created_by, created_at). affected_inventory (id, recall_id FK, sku_id, store_location, unit_count, identified_at). affected_customers (id, recall_id FK, customer_id FK, email_encrypted, phone_encrypted, purchase_date, notification_status ENUM pending/sent/failed/undeliverable). warranty_registrations (id, customer_id, product_model, serial_number, purchase_date, customer_email_encrypted, customer_phone_encrypted, customer_address_encrypted). notification_batches (id, recall_id FK, template_id FK, approved_by, approved_at, dispatch_status ENUM draft/approved/dispatching/completed, total_recipients, sent_count, failed_count). cpsc_reports (id, recall_id FK, report_type, generated_at, pdf_s3_key, submission_status, deadline_date). compliance_audit_log (id, user_id, customer_id, action, fields_accessed_json, purpose, ip_address, created_at).
data.retention_and_deletion: PII in warranty_registrations purged 7 years post-warranty expiry via monthly batch job; product_recalls and cpsc_reports retained indefinitely; notification delivery logs retained 10 years

devops.deployment_topology: Azure App Service Plan (P2v3) running Node.js API and Next.js SSR; Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server with HA; Azure Blob Storage for CPSC PDF archive; Azure Communication Services for email and SMS
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps Pipelines: eslint + TypeScript strict, Jest 80% coverage gate, build, deploy to staging, integration smoke tests (recall creation, affected lookup, PDF generation), legal sign-off gate required before production deploy of notification templates
devops.observability_stack: Azure Application Insights with Node.js SDK; structured JSON logs to Log Analytics workspace; custom metric: notification_batch_dispatch_duration_ms, cpsc_report_generation_ms, affected_lookup_duration_seconds
devops.rollback_strategy: App Service deployment slots (staging/production) with swap; PostgreSQL point-in-time restore for data issues; failed notification batches are idempotent on retry

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a CPSC-compliant recall management platform on Azure App Service with Node.js. Key decisions: affected customer identification as async background job (not synchronous query) to avoid timeouts on large warranty databases; column-level pgcrypto encryption for customer PII with Azure Key Vault key management; notification batch approval gate enforcing compliance_officer sign-off before any PII use in outbound messages; pre-aggregated recall summary data for 15-minute CPSC report SLA.
target_output.title: Product Recall & Warranty Compliance Management Platform
target_output.executive_summary: An Azure App Service-hosted web application for a consumer electronics retailer managing the full product recall lifecycle: incident creation, automatic affected inventory and customer identification, compliance officer-gated notification dispatch via Azure Communication Services, and CPSC Form 3554-compliant PDF report generation. Customer PII in warranty registrations is encrypted at column level with pgcrypto and Azure Key Vault. Compliance deadline tracking surfaces CPSC filing countdowns with 72h/48h/24h email alerts to prevent regulatory violations.
target_output.architecture_overview: Two-tier architecture: Next.js 14 SSR frontend and Node.js Express REST API co-deployed on Azure App Service Plan P2v3. PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server with HA stores recall records, warranty registrations (PII column-encrypted), and compliance audit log. Azure Blob Storage archives generated CPSC PDF reports. Azure Communication Services handles email and SMS notification dispatch. Affected customer identification runs as a background job triggered on recall creation, writing results to affected_customers and affected_inventory tables with Server-Sent Events progress streaming to the frontend. A daily cron job monitors cpsc_reports.deadline_date and sends internal deadline alerts to compliance officers at 72h, 48h, and 24h thresholds.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 14, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, React Query, Server-Sent Events client. Backend: Node.js 22, Express with TypeScript, Zod for request validation, PDFKit for CPSC report generation, node-cron for deadline alerts. Database: PostgreSQL 16 with pgcrypto for column-level PII encryption; Azure Key Vault for symmetric encryption key. Notifications: Azure Communication Services Email and SMS. Auth: Azure AD B2C with MSAL. Storage: Azure Blob for CPSC PDF archive. CI/CD: Azure DevOps Pipelines.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Recall Creation & Scoping: compliance officer creates recall record with product model, affected date range, hazard description; triggers async affected inventory and warranty customer identification. Affected Lookup Engine: background job queries ERP inventory API and warranty_registrations table, populates affected_inventory and affected_customers with notification_status=pending. Notification Batch Management: CS manager drafts notification batch using legal-approved templates; compliance officer reviews and approves; dispatch job sends to affected customers in 500-recipient chunks tracking per-recipient delivery. CPSC Report Generation: PDFKit renders CPSC Form 3554 fields from recall record and notification delivery stats; PDF stored in Azure Blob with generation timestamp; must complete within 15 minutes. Compliance Deadline Tracker: dashboard with countdown timers for CPSC filing deadlines; daily cron fires 72h/48h/24h pre-deadline alerts to compliance_officer role emails. Remediation Action Tracker: records per-customer remediation outcome (refund_issued, replacement_shipped, repair_completed).
target_output.system_components: Azure App Service Plan P2v3 running Node.js/Express API and Next.js SSR server. Azure Database for PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server with HA and automated daily backups. Azure Blob Storage for CPSC PDF archive (immutable storage tier for regulatory documents). Azure Communication Services Email for customer notifications and internal deadline alerts; ACS SMS for high-urgency recall notifications. Azure AD B2C tenant for customer-facing auth and internal staff auth. Azure Key Vault for pgcrypto symmetric key management. Azure Monitor + Application Insights for structured logging and APM. Azure DevOps Pipelines for CI/CD.
target_output.workflows: Recall Activation Workflow: Compliance officer creates recall record specifying product_model, affected_date_range, hazard_description; API writes product_recalls record with status=draft; triggers async affected lookup job; job queries ERP REST API for affected inventory units by SKU and queries warranty_registrations for customers who purchased affected models in the date range; progress streamed to frontend via SSE; compliance officer reviews affected counts, promotes recall to status=active. Notification Dispatch Workflow: CS manager opens approved recall, selects legal-approved notification template, creates draft notification_batch; compliance officer reviews recipient list (PII displayed only after compliance_audit_log entry written) and clicks Approve; dispatch job processes batch in chunks of 500 via ACS Email and ACS SMS; per-recipient delivery status written to notification_delivery_log; failures retried up to 3 times; final delivery summary visible in recall detail. CPSC Report Generation: Compliance officer opens recall detail, clicks Generate CPSC Report; API assembles recall record fields, affected unit counts, notification delivery statistics into CPSC Form 3554 structure; PDFKit renders PDF in under 15 minutes; PDF uploaded to Azure Blob immutable tier; download link and generation timestamp recorded in cpsc_reports.
target_output.data_model: product_recalls (id UUID PK, recall_number VARCHAR UNIQUE, product_model VARCHAR, product_description TEXT, hazard_description TEXT, affected_date_start DATE, affected_date_end DATE, status ENUM draft/active/reporting/closed, affected_inventory_count INT, affected_customer_count INT, cpsc_submission_status ENUM pending/submitted/acknowledged, created_by UUID FK, created_at, updated_at). affected_inventory (id UUID PK, recall_id FK, sku_id, store_location_id, unit_count INT, identified_at). affected_customers (id UUID PK, recall_id FK, warranty_registration_id FK, notification_status ENUM pending/sent/failed/undeliverable, remediation_status ENUM pending/refund_issued/replacement_shipped/repair_completed, identified_at). warranty_registrations (id UUID PK, customer_id UUID, product_model VARCHAR, serial_number VARCHAR, purchase_date DATE, email_encrypted BYTEA, phone_encrypted BYTEA, address_encrypted BYTEA, registered_at). notification_batches (id UUID PK, recall_id FK, template_id FK, created_by UUID, approved_by UUID, approved_at, dispatch_status ENUM draft/approved/dispatching/completed, total_recipients INT, sent_count INT, failed_count INT). cpsc_reports (id UUID PK, recall_id FK, report_type VARCHAR, generated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, pdf_blob_key VARCHAR, submission_status, deadline_date DATE). compliance_audit_log (id UUID PK, user_id FK, customer_id, action VARCHAR, fields_accessed_json JSONB, purpose VARCHAR, ip_address INET, created_at) -- append-only.
target_output.api_design: REST at /api/v1. POST /recalls (compliance_officer; creates recall, triggers async affected lookup). GET /recalls (paginated list with deadline countdown). GET /recalls/:id (full recall with affected counts and notification status). GET /recalls/:id/affected-customers (paginated; requires compliance_audit_log entry write for PII fields). GET /recalls/:id/affected-lookup/progress (SSE stream of affected lookup progress). POST /notifications/batches body {recall_id, template_id} (creates draft batch). GET /notifications/batches/:id (batch status with delivery summary). PATCH /notifications/batches/:id/approve (compliance_officer only). POST /notifications/batches/:id/dispatch (compliance_officer; starts dispatch job). POST /recalls/:id/cpsc-report (compliance_officer; triggers PDF generation). GET /recalls/:id/cpsc-report (download link). All routes require Azure AD B2C JWT; RBAC enforced per route.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Azure AD B2C with MFA enforced for all roles. Express middleware validates JWT and extracts role claim; RBAC checked per route. Column-level encryption: warranty_registrations.email, phone, address fields encrypted with pgcrypto using symmetric key from Azure Key Vault; fields decrypted in application layer only when needed for notification dispatch. Every PII field decryption event writes a compliance_audit_log entry with user_id, customer_id, fields_accessed, purpose, and IP. Notification dispatch approval gate: no customer PII can be used in outbound communications without compliance_officer PATCH /approve action. CPSC PDF reports stored in Azure Blob immutable storage tier -- blobs cannot be deleted or modified after upload. TLS 1.3 enforced at Azure Front Door.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Azure DevOps Pipelines CI: eslint, TypeScript strict, Jest unit tests 80% coverage gate (recall state machine, CPSC report field mapping, notification batch workflow), build, deploy to staging App Service slot, integration smoke tests (recall creation and affected lookup, PDF generation under 15 minutes, notification batch approval flow), legal sign-off required gate before production swap of notification template changes. Production: Azure App Service P2v3 with deployment slot swap (zero-downtime deploys). PostgreSQL Flexible Server automated daily backups with 35-day retention and point-in-time restore. Alembic-style migrations via db-migrate npm package run as pre-deploy step. Azure Blob immutable storage tier for CPSC PDFs with 10-year retention lock.
target_output.observability: Azure Application Insights: affected_lookup_duration_seconds (by product_model cardinality), notification_batch_dispatch_duration_ms, cpsc_report_generation_ms (must be <900000 for 15-minute SLA), notification_delivery_failure_rate. Azure Monitor alerts: affected lookup job running >10 minutes (may indicate ERP API timeout), CPSC report generation >12 minutes (SLA risk warning), notification batch failure rate >5%. Structured JSON logs include recall_id, action, user_id on every request for compliance traceability. Compliance deadline email alerts sent by node-cron at 72h/48h/24h before cpsc_reports.deadline_date.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $550-750/month Azure: App Service P2v3 ~$250, PostgreSQL Flexible Server ~$200, ACS email + SMS per recall event ~$50-100 depending on affected customer volume, Blob Storage ~$20, Application Insights ~$30. At high recall volume, scale App Service to P3v3 and add PostgreSQL read replica; notification dispatch can be moved to Azure Service Bus queue-based processing for higher throughput.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (14 weeks, legal sign-off gates): Azure AD B2C RBAC, recall creation workflow, async affected inventory and warranty customer identification, notification batch drafting and approval, email notification dispatch via ACS, basic CPSC PDF export with Form 3554 fields, compliance deadline tracker, compliance_audit_log for PII access. Phase 2 (8 weeks): SMS notification dispatch, per-recipient delivery status tracking with retry, remediation outcome tracking, CPSC report submission status tracking, advanced deadline alert escalation. Phase 3 (8 weeks): CPSC Recalls Portal electronic filing API integration, postal mail notification support, supplier recall cost recovery module, multi-recall bulk management for product families.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict on both Next.js and Express layers. Zod schemas for all API request bodies and CPSC report field mappings -- legal-reviewed field mappings must not be changed without legal sign-off ADR. Jest unit tests for recall state machine transitions, CPSC PDF field assembly, and notification batch approval workflow logic. 80% coverage gate. pgcrypto key access wrapped in a KeyVaultService class; raw key material must never appear in application logs or error messages. Compliance audit log writes must succeed before any PII decryption; if audit log write fails, return 500 without decrypting.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Affected customer lookup query times out on large warranty_registrations tables (>10M rows) for popular product models. Mitigation: background async job with SSE progress streaming; index on warranty_registrations(product_model, purchase_date). Risk: CPSC report generation exceeds 15-minute SLA during high-load periods. Mitigation: pre-aggregate recall summary stats in product_recalls table updated by background jobs; PDFKit render uses pre-computed values not live queries. Tradeoff: Azure App Service over AKS -- simpler operations at this scale, $400/month cheaper, sufficient for moderate concurrent usage. Tradeoff: column-level pgcrypto over application-layer encryption -- reduces key management complexity but requires pgcrypto extension and Azure Key Vault integration.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: pgcrypto symmetric encryption over application-layer AES chosen to keep encryption co-located with the database transaction boundary. ACS Email over SendGrid chosen to stay within Azure ecosystem and existing enterprise agreement. PDFKit over Puppeteer for CPSC report generation -- PDFKit is lighter and has no browser dependency in the App Service environment. Async affected lookup over synchronous query chosen to avoid 30-second Express timeout for recalls affecting millions of warranty registrations.

metadata.notes: CPSC recall compliance platform on Azure App Service with PII column encryption, notification approval gating, and CPSC Form 3554 PDF generation.

profile.domain: Independent Retail - Mobile Inventory & Sales Management

project_goal.value: Mobile application for independent boutique retailers to manage product inventory, track sales, and receive low-stock alerts from their smartphone with offline-first capability for poor connectivity environments
project_goal.rationale: Boutique owners frequently operate in areas with intermittent connectivity; offline-first is a non-negotiable core capability

target_users.value: Independent boutique store owners and their sales staff
target_users.rationale: Non-technical primary users; simple UX and reliable offline behavior are essential

project_class.rationale: Native mobile experience with push notifications and offline-first SQLite required

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, analytics, payments
capabilities.rationale: Mobile app with offline sync, analytics dashboards, and in-app payment processing for POS usage

complexity_level.rationale: WatermelonDB offline sync, conflict resolution for concurrent edits, push notification triggers, and Stripe payment integration add meaningful complexity

risk_level.rationale: Payment card data processed via Stripe; financial sales data and customer purchase history stored

mvp_scope.rationale: Single-store MVP validates core workflow before multi-store expansion

security_baseline.value: Firebase Auth with email/password and Google SSO, TLS 1.3, no raw card data stored (Stripe tokenization), local SQLite encrypted via SQLCipher, server-side data encrypted at rest
security_baseline.rationale: PCI-DSS scope reduction via Stripe tokenization; SQLCipher protects offline data on device

frontend_stack.value: React Native with TypeScript (Expo managed workflow)
frontend_stack.rationale: Expo managed workflow for fast iteration; React Native for cross-platform iOS and Android

backend_stack.value: Node.js 22 with Fastify
backend_stack.rationale: Fastify for high-throughput sync API; Node.js team expertise

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 server-side; SQLite via WatermelonDB on device
data_platform.rationale: WatermelonDB provides offline-first SQLite with built-in sync protocol; PostgreSQL as source of truth

hosting_target.value: DigitalOcean App Platform
hosting_target.rationale: Simple managed hosting; cost-effective for MVP and early growth stage

privacy_retention_policy.value: Sales transaction records retained 7 years per accounting requirements; product catalog data retained while store subscription active; customer purchase history deleted on account cancellation within 30 days
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Accounting retention requirements and GDPR right-to-erasure on account cancellation

future_scope.value: Multi-store inventory consolidation view, e-commerce Shopify sync, customer loyalty program, team scheduling
future_scope.rationale: Roadmap items after single-store MVP validated

constraints.rationale: 48-hour offline window is the stated connectivity scenario; Stripe TTI required by product spec; budget constraint

observability_baseline.rationale: Sentry for mobile crash visibility; Datadog for API and sync performance

execution_preference.rationale: EAS Build automates iOS and Android build pipeline

project.goal: Offline-first mobile POS and inventory app for boutique retailers with Stripe payments and low-stock push alerts
project.target_users: Boutique store owners and sales staff
project.timeline: MVP in 12 weeks on TestFlight and Google Play Internal Testing

frontend.state: WatermelonDB reactive queries for inventory and sales; Zustand for UI and auth state
frontend.payments: Stripe React Native SDK with Tap to Pay on iPhone

security.auth: Firebase Auth JWT verified on Fastify
security.payments: Stripe tokenization; no raw card data

data.cache: None

constraints.payments: Stripe Tap to Pay on iPhone

reasoner_reviews.product: The low-stock alert push notification is a high-value feature for boutique owners who are on the floor and cannot monitor a dashboard. The alert must fire immediately when an adjustment or sale takes the stock below the threshold -- not on a polling schedule. Ensure the backend triggers the Expo push notification synchronously after each stock_adjustment or sale_transaction write that causes a threshold crossing.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The WatermelonDB sync conflict resolution strategy needs explicit documentation. For concurrent offline edits to the same product_listing (e.g., owner on iPad and staff on iPhone both adjust stock while offline), the server must apply last-writer-wins by updated_at timestamp for most fields but use additive merge for stock_adjustments.quantity to avoid silent data loss. Confirm this is modeled correctly in the sync protocol design.
reasoner_reviews.security: SQLCipher encryption of the local WatermelonDB database is critical for devices that could be lost or stolen in a retail environment. The encryption key must be derived from the user's Firebase Auth UID via PBKDF2 -- never stored in AsyncStorage in plaintext. Verify that the Expo SQLite + SQLCipher integration supports key derivation correctly before committing.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 48-hour offline window for sales and stock adjustments creates a merge complexity risk. If a boutique sells all units of a product while offline and the owner adds more stock via another device that syncs in the meantime, the merge must resolve without creating negative stock. Design the sync endpoint to validate post-merge stock levels and surface conflicts to the user via an in-app conflict resolution UI rather than silently applying incorrect values.

backend.service_design: Fastify TypeScript server with plugins: sync (WatermelonDB pullChanges/pushChanges), sales (transaction recording, Stripe webhook handling), notifications (low-stock alert dispatch via Expo Push API), auth (Firebase JWT middleware)
backend.api_patterns: GET /sync/pull?last_pulled_at= (returns changed records since timestamp for store_id). POST /sync/push body {changes: {product_listings, stock_adjustments, sale_transactions}} (applies changes with server-side conflict resolution, returns final server state). POST /sales/stripe-webhook (handles payment_intent.succeeded). POST /notifications/low-stock-check (internal endpoint called after each stock write)
backend.failure_handling: Sync push failures are non-destructive: server validates post-merge stock levels; if negative stock would result, returns 409 with conflict detail for client-side resolution UI. Stripe webhook idempotency via event ID deduplication in processed_webhook_events table

frontend.app_structure: Expo managed workflow with React Navigation for tab and stack navigation; WatermelonDB database initialized with SQLCipher key on app launch
frontend.pages_and_flows: Home (stock health summary, recent sales), Product Catalog (WatermelonDB reactive list with barcode scan, stock indicators), Sale Recording (product search, Stripe Tap to Pay, receipt confirmation), Stock Adjustments (receive stock, manual count correction, reason notes), Analytics (weekly sales chart, top-selling products, low-stock list), Settings (low-stock thresholds per product, supplier contacts, store profile)
frontend.state_management: WatermelonDB @lazy queries for product_listings and sale_transactions render reactively; Zustand for auth state, active cart, and sync status indicator
frontend.performance_notes: WatermelonDB lazy loading for product catalog >500 SKUs; Expo Camera for barcode scan; offline indicator banner displayed when NetInfo.isConnected is false

security.auth_design: Firebase Auth with email/password and Google SSO; Firebase ID token sent as Bearer in Fastify API calls; Fastify firebase-admin middleware verifies token and attaches uid and store_id claim
security.authorization_model: RBAC: owner JWT claim allows all store records; staff claim includes store_id restriction enforced on sync pull/push to prevent cross-store data access
security.secrets_management: SQLCipher key derived from Firebase UID via PBKDF2 with device salt stored in iOS Keychain / Android Keystore -- never in AsyncStorage; Stripe publishable key bundled in app; secret key in DigitalOcean App Platform env vars

data.entities: product_listings (id, store_id, sku, name, barcode, selling_price, cost_price, current_stock INT, reorder_threshold INT, supplier_contact_id, last_modified_at) -- synced. stock_adjustments (id, product_id, store_id, adjustment_type ENUM receive/correction/sale/return, quantity_delta INT, reason_note, performed_by, created_at) -- synced, additive merge. sale_transactions (id, store_id, stripe_payment_intent_id, total_amount, items_json, performed_by, synced_at, created_at) -- synced. low_stock_alerts (id, product_id, store_id, alert_type ENUM below_threshold/out_of_stock, triggered_at, expo_push_token, delivered BOOL). sync_queue_items (id, table_name, record_id, operation ENUM create/update/delete, payload_json, created_at, synced_at) -- local only.
data.storage_design: WatermelonDB with SQLCipher on device for offline-first storage; PostgreSQL 16 as server source of truth; sync_queue_items table tracks pending changes when offline

devops.deployment_topology: DigitalOcean App Platform: Fastify API component + PostgreSQL 16 managed database + background worker for low-stock alert dispatch
devops.ci_cd_design: Buildkite: eslint + TypeScript, Jest unit tests 80% coverage for sync conflict resolution logic, Fastify API tests against PostgreSQL test DB; Expo EAS Build for iOS (TestFlight) and Android (Internal Testing) on PR merge to main
devops.observability_stack: Sentry React Native SDK for crash reporting and performance tracing; Datadog agent on App Platform for sync_push_duration_ms, sale_transaction_rate, push_notification_delivery_rate
devops.rollback_strategy: DigitalOcean App Platform instant rollback to prior deployment; PostgreSQL point-in-time restore; WatermelonDB schema migrations versioned and backward-compatible

ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan stored SQLCipher encryption key in AsyncStorage which is not encrypted on Android; resolved in round 2 by deriving key from Firebase UID via PBKDF2 stored in iOS Keychain / Android Keystore

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 addresses ISS-002 (negative stock from concurrent offline merges) and ISS-003 (polling-based low-stock alerts too slow). ISS-002 fixed by adding post-merge stock validation in the sync push handler with 409 conflict response and client conflict resolution UI. ISS-003 fixed by triggering low-stock check synchronously after each stock-reducing write in the sync push handler and dispatching Expo push notification inline. These changes affect the sync protocol, workflows, and notification design sections.

fix_report.action_taken: Added post-merge stock validation step in the sync push handler: after applying all stock_adjustments from the push payload, queries the resulting product_listings.current_stock for each affected product; if any product would result in current_stock < 0, returns HTTP 409 with conflict_type='negative_stock', affected_product_ids, and current_server_stock_per_product. Client handles 409 by displaying a conflict resolution modal showing the conflicting product, server stock value, and offline adjustment, allowing user to choose Keep Server Value or Adjust Offline Sale.
fix_report.expected_outcome: No silent negative stock values; boutique owners see explicit conflict when their offline sell-down exceeded actual available stock; data integrity preserved

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced 5-minute polling cron with synchronous low-stock check triggered inline in the sync push handler after each stock_adjustment or sale_transaction write. After applying changes, handler queries products WHERE current_stock <= reorder_threshold AND store_id = ?; for each threshold-crossing product, issues Expo Push API call within the same request handler. Low-stock push notification delivered within seconds of the triggering sync.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Low-stock alerts delivered within the sync round-trip (~2 seconds) rather than up to 5 minutes; boutique owners notified immediately during busy selling periods

target_output.title: Boutique Inventory & POS Mobile App
target_output.executive_summary: A React Native Expo offline-first mobile application for independent boutique retailers managing product inventory, recording sales via Stripe Tap to Pay, and receiving real-time low-stock push alerts. WatermelonDB with SQLCipher (PBKDF2 key from Keychain/Keystore) provides encrypted local storage for 48-hour offline operation. A Fastify sync API handles conflict-safe pushChanges with post-merge stock validation, returning 409 on negative-stock conflicts with data for a client-side resolution UI. Low-stock alerts are dispatched synchronously in the sync push handler, delivering notifications within seconds of a threshold-crossing sale.
target_output.architecture_overview: Offline-first React Native app with WatermelonDB SQLite (SQLCipher encrypted) as the device-local database syncing to PostgreSQL 16 via a Fastify sync API on DigitalOcean App Platform. The sync pull endpoint returns changed records since last_pulled_at; the sync push endpoint applies device changes with server-side validation: (1) last-writer-wins by last_modified_at for product_listing fields, (2) additive merge for stock_adjustment.quantity_delta, (3) post-merge stock level validation rejecting any push that would produce negative stock with a 409 conflict response. Stripe Tap to Pay on iPhone handles contactless payment tokenization. Expo Push Service delivers low-stock notifications dispatched synchronously from the sync push handler after each stock-reducing write. Firebase Auth JWT validated on every Fastify request.
target_output.technology_stack: Mobile: React Native with TypeScript, Expo SDK 51, WatermelonDB 0.27 with SQLCipher, Zustand, React Navigation 6, Stripe React Native SDK with Tap to Pay, Expo Notifications, Expo Camera (barcode scan), NetInfo for connectivity detection. Backend: Node.js 22, Fastify 4 with TypeScript, fastify-firebase-auth plugin, node-postgres (pg), Expo Server SDK for push notifications. Database: PostgreSQL 16 (DigitalOcean managed), WatermelonDB SQLite on device. Security: SQLCipher 4, PBKDF2 key derivation, iOS Keychain / Android Keystore via expo-secure-store. CI/CD: Buildkite, Expo EAS Build.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Offline-First Product Catalog: WatermelonDB reactive @lazy query renders product_listings with real-time stock indicators; barcode scan via Expo Camera resolves product lookup offline. Sale Recording: cart with product search, Stripe Tap to Pay contactless flow, receipt confirmation; sale_transaction written to WatermelonDB locally and queued for sync. Stock Adjustment: receive stock, manual correction, return processing; adjustment_type enum enforces business rule (receive adds, sale subtracts, correction sets). Sync Engine: automatic sync on reconnect via NetInfo; sync push handler applies changes with post-merge stock validation; conflict resolution UI for negative-stock 409 responses. Low-Stock Alerts: push notification dispatched synchronously from sync push handler when current_stock <= reorder_threshold after any stock-reducing change. Sales Analytics: WatermelonDB queries aggregate daily/weekly sales totals and top-selling products rendered with React Native Charts Kit. Supplier Quick-Reorder: one-tap deep link to pre-filled WhatsApp or email message to configured supplier contact.
target_output.system_components: React Native Expo app (iOS and Android) with WatermelonDB SQLite database (SQLCipher encrypted, key from expo-secure-store PBKDF2 derivation). Fastify API on DigitalOcean App Platform with sync, sales, auth, and notifications route plugins. PostgreSQL 16 managed database on DigitalOcean. Expo Push Service for push notification delivery (token management, delivery confirmation). Stripe Dashboard and webhook endpoint for payment_intent.succeeded confirmation. Firebase Auth project for user authentication and UID-based SQLCipher key derivation. Sentry React Native SDK for crash reporting. Datadog agent on App Platform for API metrics. Buildkite CI + Expo EAS Build for iOS/Android build and distribution.
target_output.workflows: Offline Sale Recording and Sync: Store owner opens app (offline), searches for product by name or barcode scan, adds to cart, processes Stripe Tap to Pay payment (Stripe SDK operates offline for card read, queues transaction); sale_transaction written to WatermelonDB with synced_at=null; stock automatically decremented in local product_listings. On connectivity restore, WatermelonDB sync triggers automatically: pullChanges fetches server updates since last_pulled_at; pushChanges sends queued sale_transactions and stock_adjustments; server applies changes with post-merge stock validation; if stock would go negative, returns 409 with conflict_type='negative_stock', server_stock_value, and conflicting_product_id; client displays conflict modal with 'Keep Server Stock' or 'Record As Override With Note' options. Low-Stock Alert Dispatch: sync push handler, after committing all changes to PostgreSQL, queries SELECT p.id, p.name, p.current_stock, p.reorder_threshold FROM product_listings p WHERE p.current_stock <= p.reorder_threshold AND p.store_id = $storeId AND changes affected p.id; for each result, issues Expo Push API call with message 'Low stock: {product_name} has {current_stock} units remaining'; push token retrieved from store_push_tokens table. Conflict Resolution UI: user sees a modal showing the product name, the server's current stock value, and their offline adjustment quantity; if they choose Keep Server Value, the local WatermelonDB record is reset to the server value; if they choose Override with Note, a manual stock_adjustment is created with reason_note='owner_override_post_conflict' and synced immediately.
target_output.data_model: product_listings (id UUID, store_id FK, sku VARCHAR, name VARCHAR, barcode VARCHAR UNIQUE, selling_price NUMERIC, cost_price NUMERIC, current_stock INT CHECK >= 0, reorder_threshold INT, supplier_contact_id FK, last_modified_at TIMESTAMPTZ) -- synced bidirectionally. stock_adjustments (id UUID, product_id FK, store_id FK, adjustment_type ENUM receive/correction/sale/return, quantity_delta INT, reason_note TEXT, performed_by UUID, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ) -- synced, additive merge by sum of quantity_delta. sale_transactions (id UUID, store_id FK, stripe_payment_intent_id VARCHAR, total_amount NUMERIC, items_json JSONB, performed_by UUID, synced_at TIMESTAMPTZ, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ) -- synced. low_stock_alerts (id UUID, product_id FK, store_id FK, current_stock_at_trigger INT, triggered_at TIMESTAMPTZ, expo_delivered BOOL). store_push_tokens (store_id FK, user_id FK, expo_push_token VARCHAR, updated_at). sync_queue_items (id UUID, table_name, record_id, operation ENUM create/update/delete, payload_json JSONB, created_at, synced_at) -- local WatermelonDB only. processed_webhook_events (stripe_event_id VARCHAR PK, processed_at TIMESTAMPTZ) -- Stripe idempotency.
target_output.api_design: Fastify REST at /api/v1. GET /sync/pull?last_pulled_at=ISO&store_id= (returns {changes: {product_listings: [], stock_adjustments: [], sale_transactions: []}, timestamp: ISO}). POST /sync/push body {changes: {product_listings: [], stock_adjustments: [], sale_transactions: []}, lastPulledAt: ISO, store_id:} (applies changes, validates post-merge stock, dispatches low-stock push notifications inline; returns 200 on success or 409 {conflict_type: 'negative_stock', conflicts: [{product_id, product_name, server_stock, requested_adjustment}]}). POST /auth/register-push-token body {expo_push_token} (stores token for authenticated user's store). POST /webhooks/stripe body (Stripe webhook with idempotency check). All routes require Firebase Auth Bearer token; store_id extracted from token claims and validated against request body.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Firebase Auth with email/password and Google SSO; Fastify fastify-firebase-auth plugin verifies ID token on every request and attaches uid + store_id. RBAC enforced in route handlers: staff token store_id must match request store_id; owner token may have multiple store_ids. SQLCipher 4 encrypts local WatermelonDB SQLite; key derived from Firebase UID via PBKDF2 with 100K iterations and per-device salt stored in iOS Keychain / Android Keystore via expo-secure-store -- key never written to AsyncStorage. Stripe Tap to Pay: card data captured and tokenized by Stripe SDK on-device; no raw PAN data reaches app code or backend; PCI SAQ A scope. No raw payment card data stored in PostgreSQL or WatermelonDB; only stripe_payment_intent_id retained. TLS 1.3 for all API communication. Stripe webhook signature validation via stripe.webhooks.constructEvent().
target_output.deployment_and_operations: DigitalOcean App Platform: Fastify API component (1x Basic-XXS, autoscale to 2x on CPU 80%) and PostgreSQL 16 managed database. Buildkite CI: eslint + TypeScript strict, Jest unit tests (sync conflict resolution logic, post-merge stock validation, low-stock trigger logic) with 80% coverage gate, integration tests against test PostgreSQL with seeded conflicting offline payloads. Expo EAS Build: iOS build targeting TestFlight on PR merge to main; Android targeting Google Play Internal Testing. WatermelonDB schema migrations via addMigrations() versioned and backward-compatible. Sentry release tracking tied to Expo app version via @sentry/react-native. DigitalOcean App Platform deploy rollback via 1-click dashboard revert.
target_output.observability: Sentry React Native: crash rate by screen and Expo SDK version; performance tracing for sync pull/push duration on device; offline duration tracking before sync triggers. Datadog on App Platform: sync_push_duration_ms (p50/p95), sync_push_conflict_rate (negative_stock 409s), low_stock_alert_dispatch_ms, stripe_webhook_processing_ms, sale_transaction_sync_lag_seconds (time from local creation to synced_at). Expo Push Service delivery confirmation: low_stock_alerts.expo_delivered updated on Expo Push receipt check (run hourly via background job). Alerts: sync_push_conflict_rate >5% in a store suggests concurrent editing workflow issue; Sentry error rate spike on new Expo SDK version.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $200-250/month: DigitalOcean App Platform Basic ~$120, managed PostgreSQL ~$50, Expo Push free tier covers boutique volumes, Stripe fees per transaction (2.9% + 30c standard). At 1000+ stores, migrate to DigitalOcean Premium tier and add PostgreSQL read replica for analytics queries; Expo Push free tier covers up to 1M notifications/month.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (12 weeks, iOS and Android TestFlight/Internal): WatermelonDB offline sync with SQLCipher PBKDF2 key, product catalog with barcode scan, stock adjustments, sale recording with Stripe Tap to Pay, sync push handler with post-merge stock validation and 409 conflict response, synchronous low-stock push notification dispatch, Firebase Auth, Buildkite + EAS Build CI. Phase 2 (8 weeks): Conflict resolution UI for negative-stock 409 responses, sales analytics dashboard, supplier quick-reorder deep links, multi-store owner dashboard. Phase 3 (8 weeks): Shopify product catalog sync, customer loyalty points, team scheduling module, Android Tap to Pay support.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict on both Expo app and Fastify backend. Jest unit tests for sync conflict resolution (additive merge logic, post-merge stock validation, negative-stock 409 path, low-stock trigger logic) are the highest-priority test suite -- 90% coverage on sync/ module. WatermelonDB schema migrations via addMigrations() must be backward-compatible and tested against the prior schema version in CI. SQLCipher key derivation logic tested in isolation with known UID, salt, and expected key output. Stripe webhook handlers tested with Stripe CLI fixture events in CI. ADR for WatermelonDB conflict resolution strategy documenting last-writer-wins vs additive merge decisions per table.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Expo SQLite + SQLCipher integration breaking on Expo SDK major version upgrades. Mitigation: pin SQLCipher version in package.json; integration test in Buildkite against device simulator before any Expo SDK upgrade PR merges. Risk: Large offline queues (48 hours of sales for a busy boutique) causing sync push timeouts. Mitigation: batch pushChanges into chunks of 100 records with sequential pushes; progress indicator shown during sync. Tradeoff: WatermelonDB over Realm -- WatermelonDB's sync protocol is more customizable for the additive merge + stock validation requirements; Realm's sync is opinionated and harder to extend. Tradeoff: DigitalOcean App Platform over AWS -- lower cost at this scale ($200/month vs $400+) with acceptable managed services quality for a startup-stage product.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Additive merge for stock_adjustments.quantity_delta selected over last-writer-wins to preserve all offline stock movements; negative-stock cases handled via 409 conflict UI rather than silent clamping. Synchronous push notification dispatch in sync push handler selected over async background worker to minimize alert latency for POS use case. expo-secure-store PBKDF2 key derivation confirmed compatible with SQLCipher via @margelo/react-native-quick-sqlite fork. Conflict resolution UI deferred to Phase 2 due to MVP timeline but 409 API contract established in Phase 1.

metadata.notes: Round 3 fixes offline stock merge negative-stock issue (ISS-002) with 409 conflict response and event-driven low-stock alert trigger (ISS-003) for boutique mobile POS app.
